351	def _apply_min_tokens_penalty(
352	    logits: torch.Tensor,
353	    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
354	) -> torch.Tensor:
355	    """Apply min_tokens penalty which sets stop tokens to -inf if min_tokens
356	        have not been generated yet
357	    """
358	    # list of indices in logits that will be set to -inf
359	    logits_to_penalize: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
360	    logits_applied = 0
361	    for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
362	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
363	        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
364	
365	        sample_indices = seq_group.sample_indices
366	        logits_applied += len(sample_indices) + len(
367	            seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
368	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
369	            continue
370	
371	        start_idx = sample_indices[0]
372	        min_tokens = sampling_params.min_tokens
373	        token_ids_to_penalize = sampling_params.all_stop_token_ids
374	        if min_tokens > 0 and token_ids_to_penalize:
375	            seqs_to_penalize: list[int] = []
376	            for j, seq_id in enumerate(seq_ids):
377	                seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
378	                if len(seq_data.output_token_ids_array) < min_tokens:
379	                    seqs_to_penalize.append(j)
380	
381	            if seqs_to_penalize:
382	                # convert to the index into logits
383	                seqs_to_penalize = [start_idx + j for j in seqs_to_penalize]
384	                # itertools.product pairs each seq index with every token id
385	                logits_to_penalize.extend(
386	                    itertools.product(seqs_to_penalize, token_ids_to_penalize))
387	
388	    if logits_to_penalize:
389	        # use zip and * to group indices along each dimension
390	        # eg. [ (1,2), (1,3), (5,6) ] -> ( (1,1,5), (2,3,6) )
391	        logits[tuple(zip(*logits_to_penalize))] = -float("inf")
392	
393	    # verifies that no rows in logits were missed unexpectedly
394	    assert logits_applied == logits.shape[0]
395	    return logits

398	def _apply_top_k_top_p(
399	    logits: torch.Tensor,
400	    p: torch.Tensor,
401	    k: torch.Tensor,
402	) -> torch.Tensor:
403	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
404	
405	    # Apply top-k.
406	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
407	    # Get all the top_k values.
408	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
409	    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
410	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
411	
412	    # Apply top-p.
413	    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
414	    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
415	    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
416	    # at least one
417	    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
418	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
419	
420	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
421	    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
422	                                                    index=logits_idx,
423	                                                    src=logits_sort)
424	    return logits
425	
426	
427	def _apply_min_p(
428	    logits: torch.Tensor,
429	    min_p: torch.Tensor,
430	) -> torch.Tensor:
431	    """
432	    Adapted from
433	    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
434	    """
435	    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
436	    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
437	    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
438	    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
439	    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
440	
441	    return logits

444	def _greedy_sample(
445	    selected_seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
446	    samples: torch.Tensor,
447	) -> SampleResultType:
448	    """Run greedy sampling on a given samples.
449	
450	    Args:
451	        selected_seq_groups: A list of sequence groups batched.
452	        samples: (num_selected_samples,) A tensor of samples. The length of
453	            samples could be smaller than selected_seq_groups if
454	            seq_group.do_sample is False.
455	    Returns:
456	        Tuple of (next_token_ids, parent_ids). The length of returned list is
457	        same as the length of selected_seq_groups. If the corresponding
458	        seq_group has do_sample=False, tuple contains ([], [])
459	    """
460	    samples_lst = samples.tolist()
461	    sample_idx = 0
462	    results: SampleResultType = []
463	    for seq_group in selected_seq_groups:
464	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
465	            results.append(([], []))
466	            continue
467	
468	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
469	        num_parent_seqs = len(seq_ids)
470	        assert num_parent_seqs == 1, (
471	            "Greedy sampling should have only one seq.")
472	        parent_ids = list(range(num_parent_seqs))
473	        next_token_ids = [samples_lst[sample_idx]]
474	        results.append((next_token_ids, parent_ids))
475	        sample_idx += num_parent_seqs
476	    return results

101

104

106	    # or (2) what will be arguments for later deferred pythonization of the

108	    deferred_sample_results_args: Optional[SampleResultArgsType] = None
109	
110	    # On-device tensor containing the sampled token ids.

115	    sampled_token_ids_cpu: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None

170	    """Samples the next tokens from the model's outputs.
171	
172	    This layer does the following:
173	    1. Discard the hidden states that are not used for sampling (i.e., all

175	    2. Compute the logits for the next tokens.
176	    3. Apply presence, frequency and repetition penalties.
177	    4. Apply temperature scaling.
178	    5. Apply top-p and top-k truncation.
179	    6. Sample the next tokens.
180	    Here, each sequence group within the batch can have different sampling
181	    parameters (e.g., sampling method, temperature, top-p, top-k, etc.).
182	
183	    The structure of the logits tensor is coupled with the seq_groups in
184	    sampling_metadata. Typically, each sequence in each seq_group has one row in
185	    logits for the next token to be sampled; however, for a seq_group with a
186	    prompt request with the prompt_logprobs sampling parameter, there are rows

427	def _apply_min_p(
428	    logits: torch.Tensor,
429	    min_p: torch.Tensor,
430	) -> torch.Tensor:
431	    """
432	    Adapted from
433	    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
434	    """
435	    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
436	    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
437	    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
438	    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
439	    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
440	
441	    return logits

33	class OpenAIBaseModel(BaseModel):
34	    # OpenAI API does allow extra fields
35	    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="allow")
36	
37	    # Cache class field names
38	    field_names: ClassVar[Optional[set[str]]] = None
39	
40	    @model_validator(mode="wrap")
41	    @classmethod
42	    def __log_extra_fields__(cls, data, handler):
43	        result = handler(data)
44	        if not isinstance(data, dict):
45	            return result
46	        field_names = cls.field_names
47	        if field_names is None:
48	            # Get all class field names and their potential aliases
49	            field_names = set()
50	            for field_name, field in cls.model_fields.items():
51	                field_names.add(field_name)
52	                if alias := getattr(field, "alias", None):
53	                    field_names.add(alias)
54	            cls.field_names = field_names
55	
56	        # Compare against both field names and aliases
57	        if any(k not in field_names for k in data):
58	            logger.warning(
59	                "The following fields were present in the request "
60	                "but ignored: %s",
61	                data.keys() - field_names,
62	            )
63	        return result

192	def get_logits_processors(processors: Optional[LogitsProcessors],
193	                          pattern: Optional[str]) -> Optional[list[Any]]:
194	    if processors and pattern:
195	        logits_processors = []
196	        for processor in processors:
197	            qualname = processor if isinstance(processor,
198	                                               str) else processor.qualname
199	            if not re.match(pattern, qualname):
200	                raise ValueError(
201	                    f"Logits processor '{qualname}' is not allowed by this "
202	                    "server. See --logits-processor-pattern engine argument "
203	                    "for more information.")
204	            try:
205	                logits_processor = resolve_obj_by_qualname(qualname)
206	            except Exception as e:
207	                raise ValueError(
208	                    f"Logits processor '{qualname}' could not be resolved: {e}"
209	                ) from e
210	            if isinstance(processor, LogitsProcessorConstructor):
211	                logits_processor = logits_processor(*processor.args or [],
212	                                                    **processor.kwargs or {})
213	            logits_processors.append(logits_processor)
214	        return logits_processors
215	    elif processors:
216	        raise ValueError(
217	            "The `logits_processors` argument is not supported by this "
218	            "server. See --logits-processor-pattern engine argugment "
219	            "for more information.")
220	    return None

10	class LogprobsLists(NamedTuple):
11	
12	    # [num_reqs, max_num_logprobs + 1]
13	    logprob_token_ids: list[list[int]]
14	    # [num_reqs, max_num_logprobs + 1]
15	    logprobs: list[list[float]]
16	    # [num_reqs]
17	    sampled_token_ranks: list[int]
18	
19	    def slice(self, start: int, end: int):
20	        return LogprobsLists(
21	            self.logprob_token_ids[start:end],
22	            self.logprobs[start:end],
23	            self.sampled_token_ranks[start:end],
24	        )
25	
26	
27	class LogprobsTensors(NamedTuple):
28	
29	    # [num_reqs, max_num_logprobs + 1]
30	    logprob_token_ids: torch.Tensor
31	    # [num_reqs, max_num_logprobs + 1]
32	    logprobs: torch.Tensor
33	    # [num_reqs]
34	    selected_token_ranks: torch.Tensor
35	
36	    def tolists(self):
37	        return LogprobsLists(
38	            self.logprob_token_ids.tolist(),
39	            self.logprobs.tolist(),
40	            self.selected_token_ranks.tolist(),
41	        )
42	
43	    @staticmethod
44	    def empty_cpu(num_positions: int,
45	                  num_tokens_per_position: int) -> "LogprobsTensors":
46	        """Create empty LogprobsTensors on CPU."""
47	
48	        logprob_token_ids = torch.empty(
49	            (num_positions, num_tokens_per_position),
50	            dtype=torch.int32,
51	            device="cpu")
52	        logprobs = torch.empty_like(logprob_token_ids, dtype=torch.float32)
53	        selected_token_ranks = torch.empty(num_positions,
54	                                           dtype=torch.int32,
55	                                           device="cpu")
56	        return LogprobsTensors(
57	            logprob_token_ids=logprob_token_ids,
58	            logprobs=logprobs,
59	            selected_token_ranks=selected_token_ranks,
60	        )

523	# torch.multinomial forces a GPU<->CPU sync.
524	# Therefore, we use an optimized implementation instead.
525	# Note that we always sample with replacement.
526	# probs will be modified in place, but this is fine, as we pass
527	# in a copy already.
528	def _multinomial(
529	    probs: torch.Tensor,
530	    num_samples: int,
531	    seq_groups: Optional[list[SequenceGroupToSample]] = None,
532	) -> torch.Tensor:
533	    if num_samples > 1:
534	        probs = probs.repeat_interleave(num_samples, dim=0)
535	    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
536	    if seq_groups is None:
537	        q.exponential_()
538	    else:
539	        sample_idx = 0
540	        for seq_group in seq_groups:
541	            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
542	            stride = len(seq_ids) * num_samples
543	            assert seq_group.generator is not None
544	            q[sample_idx:sample_idx +
545	              stride].exponential_(generator=seq_group.generator)
546	            sample_idx += stride
547	    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=1).view(-1, num_samples)

550	def _top_k_top_p_multinomial_with_flashinfer(
551	        probs: torch.Tensor, top_ks: torch.Tensor, top_ps: torch.Tensor,
552	        num_samples: int, seq_groups: Optional[list[SequenceGroupToSample]]):
553	    if num_samples > 1:
554	        probs = probs.repeat_interleave(num_samples, dim=0)
555	        top_ks = top_ks.repeat_interleave(num_samples)
556	        top_ps = top_ps.repeat_interleave(num_samples)
557	    batch_next_token_ids = flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling(
558	        probs,
559	        top_ks,
560	        top_ps,
561	    )
562	    return batch_next_token_ids.view(-1, num_samples)

566	        sample_result_args: SampleResultArgsType) -> SampleResultType:
567	    '''This function consumes GPU-side sampler results and computes
568	    Pythonized CPU-side sampler results (GPU -> CPU sync.)
569	
570	    Single-step scheduling: this function is invoked at sampling-time
571	    for immediate Pythonization.
572	
573	    Multi-step scheduling: Pythonization is deferred until after multiple
574	    GPU-side steps have been completed.
575	
576	    Args:
577	      sample_result_args: GPU-side inputs to the Pythonization process
578	
579	    Returns:
580	      Pythonized sampler results
581	    '''

583	    (
584	        sample_metadata,
585	        sampling_metadata,
586	        greedy_samples,
587	        multinomial_samples,
588	        sample_results_dict,
589	    ) = (
590	        sample_result_args.sample_metadata,
591	        sample_result_args.sampling_metadata,
592	        sample_result_args.greedy_samples,
593	        sample_result_args.multinomial_samples,
594	        sample_result_args.sample_results_dict,
595	    )
596	
597	    for sampling_type in SamplingType:
598	        if sampling_type not in sample_metadata:
599	            continue
600	        (seq_group_id, seq_groups) = sample_metadata[sampling_type]
601	        if sampling_type == SamplingType.GREEDY:
602	            sample_results = _greedy_sample(seq_groups, greedy_samples)
603	        elif sampling_type in (SamplingType.RANDOM, SamplingType.RANDOM_SEED):
604	            sample_results = _random_sample(seq_groups,
605	                                            multinomial_samples[sampling_type])
606	        sample_results_dict.update(zip(seq_group_id, sample_results))
607	
608	    return [
609	        sample_results_dict.get(i, ([], []))
610	        for i in range(len(sampling_metadata.seq_groups))
611	    ]

386	    @classmethod
387	    def from_sampling_metadata(
388	        cls,
389	        sampling_metadata: "SamplingMetadata",
390	        vocab_size: int,
391	        device: torch.device,
392	        dtype: torch.dtype,
393	    ) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool]:
394	        prompt_tokens: list[array] = []
395	        output_tokens: list[array] = []
396	        top_ks: list[int] = []
397	        temperatures: list[float] = []
398	        top_ps: list[float] = []
399	        min_ps: list[float] = []
400	        presence_penalties: list[float] = []
401	        frequency_penalties: list[float] = []
402	        repetition_penalties: list[float] = []
403	        do_penalties = False
404	        do_top_p_top_k = False
405	        do_min_p = False
406	
407	        assert sampling_metadata.seq_groups is not None
408	        for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
409	            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
410	            sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
411	            temperature = sampling_params.temperature
412	            p = sampling_params.presence_penalty
413	            f = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
414	            r = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
415	            top_p = sampling_params.top_p
416	            min_p = sampling_params.min_p
417	
418	            # k should not be greater than the vocab size.
419	            top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
420	            top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
421	            if temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
422	                # NOTE: Zero temperature means deterministic sampling
423	                # (i.e., greedy sampling or beam search).
424	                # Set the temperature to 1 to avoid division by zero.
425	                temperature = 1.0
426	            if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
427	                                       or top_k != vocab_size):
428	                do_top_p_top_k = True
429	            if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
430	                do_min_p = True
431	            if not do_penalties and (abs(p) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
432	                                     or abs(f) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
433	                                     or abs(r - 1.0) >= _SAMPLING_EPS):
434	                do_penalties = True
435	
436	            is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
437	            if is_prompt and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
438	                # For tokens in the prompt that we only need to get
439	                # their logprobs
440	                query_len = seq_group.query_len
441	                assert query_len is not None
442	                prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
443	                temperatures += [temperature] * prefill_len
444	                top_ps += [top_p] * prefill_len
445	                top_ks += [top_k] * prefill_len
446	                min_ps += [min_p] * prefill_len
447	                presence_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
448	                frequency_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
449	                repetition_penalties += [1] * prefill_len
450	
451	            if seq_group.do_sample:
452	                sample_lens = len(seq_group.sample_indices)
453	                assert sample_lens >= len(seq_ids)
454	                temperatures += [temperature] * sample_lens
455	                top_ps += [top_p] * sample_lens
456	                top_ks += [top_k] * sample_lens
457	                min_ps += [min_p] * sample_lens
458	                presence_penalties += [p] * sample_lens
459	                frequency_penalties += [f] * sample_lens
460	                repetition_penalties += [r] * sample_lens
461	
462	        if do_penalties:
463	            for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
464	                seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
465	                sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
466	                if (seq_group.is_prompt
467	                        and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None):
468	                    prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
469	                    prompt_tokens.extend(
470	                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
471	                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
472	                    output_tokens.extend(
473	                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
474	                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
475	                if seq_group.do_sample:
476	                    for seq_id in seq_ids:
477	                        seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
478	                        prompt_tokens.append(seq_data.prompt_token_ids_array)
479	                        output_tokens.append(seq_data.output_token_ids_array)
480	
481	        sampling_tensors = SamplingTensors.from_lists(
482	            temperatures,
483	            top_ps,
484	            top_ks,
485	            min_ps,
486	            presence_penalties,
487	            frequency_penalties,
488	            repetition_penalties,
489	            prompt_tokens,
490	            output_tokens,
491	            vocab_size,
492	            device,
493	            dtype,
494	        )
495	        return (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p)

37	@dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)
38	class BatchUpdate:
39	    """Persistent batch state change info for logitsprocs"""
40	    batch_size: int  # Current num reqs in batch
41	
42	    # Metadata for requests added to, removed from, and moved
43	    # within the persistent batch.
44	    #
45	    # Note: each added request is represented as
46	    # (index, params, output_tok_ids)
47	    # Key assumption: output_tok_ids is a reference to the
48	    # request's running output tokens list; in this way
49	    # the logits processors always see the latest list of
50	    # generated tokens
51	    removed: Sequence[RemovedRequest]
52	    moved: Sequence[MovedRequest]
53	    added: Sequence[AddedRequest]

56	class BatchUpdateBuilder:
57	    """Helps track persistent batch state changes and build
58	    a batch update data structure for logitsprocs
59	    
60	    Assumptions:
61	    * All information about requests removed from persistent batch
62	      during a step is aggregated in self._removed through calls to
63	      self.removed_append() at the beginning of a step. This must happen
64	      before the first time that self.removed, self.pop_removed()
65	      or self.peek_removed() are invoked in a given step
66	    * After the first time that self.removed, self.pop_removed()
67	      or self.peek_removed() are read in a step, no new removals
68	      are registered using self.removed_append()
69	    * Elements of self._removed are never directly modified, added or
70	      removed (i.e. modification is only via self.removed_append() and
71	      self.pop_removed())
72	    
73	    Guarantees under above assumptions:
74	    * self.removed is always sorted in descending order
75	    * self.pop_removed() and self.peek_removed() both return
76	      the lowest removed request index in the current step
77	    """
78	
79	    _removed: list[RemovedRequest]
80	    _is_removed_sorted: bool
81	    moved: list[MovedRequest]
82	    added: list[AddedRequest]
83	
84	    def __init__(
85	        self,
86	        removed: Optional[list[RemovedRequest]] = None,
87	        moved: Optional[list[MovedRequest]] = None,
88	        added: Optional[list[AddedRequest]] = None,
89	    ) -> None:
90	        self._removed = removed or []
91	        self.moved = moved or []
92	        self.added = added or []
93	        self._is_removed_sorted = False
94	
95	    def _ensure_removed_sorted(self) -> None:
96	        """Sort removed request indices in
97	        descending order.
98	        
99	        Idempotent after first call in a
100	        given step, until reset.
101	        """
102	        if not self._is_removed_sorted:
103	            self._removed.sort(reverse=True)
104	            self._is_removed_sorted = True
105	
106	    @property
107	    def removed(self) -> list[RemovedRequest]:
108	        """Removed request indices sorted in
109	        descending order"""
110	        self._ensure_removed_sorted()
111	        return self._removed
112	
113	    def removed_append(self, index: int) -> None:
114	        """Register the removal of a request from
115	        the persistent batch.
116	
117	        Must not be called after the first time
118	        self.removed, self.pop_removed() or
119	        self.peek_removed() are invoked.
120	        
121	        Args:
122	          index: request index
123	        """
124	        if self._is_removed_sorted:
125	            raise RuntimeError("Cannot register new removed request after"
126	                               " self.removed has been read.")
127	        self._removed.append(index)
128	
129	    def has_removed(self) -> bool:
130	        return bool(self._removed)
131	
132	    def peek_removed(self) -> Optional[int]:
133	        """Return lowest removed request index"""
134	        if self.has_removed():
135	            self._ensure_removed_sorted()
136	            return self._removed[-1]
137	        return None
138	
139	    def pop_removed(self) -> Optional[int]:
140	        """Pop lowest removed request index"""
141	        if self.has_removed():
142	            self._ensure_removed_sorted()
143	            return self._removed.pop()
144	        return None
145	
146	    def get_and_reset(self, batch_size: int) -> Optional[BatchUpdate]:
147	        """Generate a logitsprocs batch update data structure
148	        and reset internal batch update builder state.
149	        
150	        Args:
151	          batch_size: current persistent batch size
152	
153	        Returns:
154	          Frozen logitsprocs batch update instance; `None` if no updates
155	        """
156	        # Reset removal-sorting logic
157	        self._is_removed_sorted = False
158	        if not any((self._removed, self.moved, self.added)):
159	            # No update; short-circuit
160	            return None
161	        # Build batch state update
162	        batch_update = BatchUpdate(
163	            batch_size=batch_size,
164	            removed=self._removed,
165	            moved=self.moved,
166	            added=self.added,
167	        )
168	        # Reset removed/moved/added update lists
169	        self._removed = []
170	        self.moved = []
171	        self.added = []
172	        return batch_update

225	class MinPLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):
226	
227	    def __init__(self, max_num_reqs: int, pin_memory: bool,
228	                 device: DeviceLikeType):
229	        super().__init__()
230	        self.min_p_count: int = 0
231	
232	        self.min_p_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros((max_num_reqs, ),
233	                                            dtype=torch.float32,
234	                                            device="cpu",
235	                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
236	        self.min_p_cpu = self.min_p_cpu_tensor.numpy()
237	        # Pre-allocated device tensor
238	        self.min_p_device: torch.Tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
239	                                                      dtype=torch.float32,
240	                                                      device=device)
241	        # Current slice of the device tensor
242	        self.min_p: torch.Tensor = self.min_p_device[:0]
243	
244	    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
245	        """Min-p never impacts greedy sampling"""
246	        return True
247	
248	    def get_min_p_by_index(self, index: int) -> float:
249	        return float(self.min_p_cpu[index])
250	
251	    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
252	        if not batch_update:
253	            return
254	
255	        needs_update = False
256	        # Process added requests.
257	        for index, params, _ in batch_update.added:
258	            min_p = params.min_p if isinstance(params, SamplingParams) else 0.0
259	            if self.min_p_cpu[index] != min_p:
260	                needs_update = True
261	                self.min_p_cpu[index] = min_p
262	            if min_p:
263	                self.min_p_count += 1
264	
265	        if self.min_p_count:
266	            # Process removed requests.
267	            needs_update |= bool(batch_update.removed)
268	            for index in batch_update.removed:
269	                if self.min_p_cpu[index]:
270	                    self.min_p_count -= 1
271	
272	            # Process moved requests, unidirectional (a->b) and swap (a<->b)
273	            for adx, bdx, direct in batch_update.moved:
274	                change = (min_p_a :=
275	                          self.min_p_cpu[adx]) != (min_p_b :=
276	                                                   self.min_p_cpu[bdx])
277	                needs_update |= change
278	                if change:
279	                    self.min_p_cpu[bdx] = min_p_a
280	                    if direct == MoveDirectionality.SWAP:
281	                        self.min_p_cpu[adx] = min_p_b
282	
283	        # Update tensors if needed.
284	        size = batch_update.batch_size
285	        if self.min_p_count and (needs_update or self.min_p.shape[0] != size):
286	            self.min_p = self.min_p_device[:size]
287	            self.min_p.copy_(self.min_p_cpu_tensor[:size], non_blocking=True)
288	            self.min_p.unsqueeze_(1)
289	
290	    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
291	        if not self.min_p_count:
292	            return logits
293	
294	        # Convert logits to probability distribution
295	        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
296	        # Calculate maximum probabilities per sequence
297	        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values,
298	                                       dim=-1,
299	                                       keepdim=True)
300	        # Adjust min_p
301	        adjusted_min_p = max_probabilities.mul_(self.min_p)
302	        # Identify valid tokens using threshold comparison
303	        invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_min_p
304	        # Apply mask using boolean indexing
305	        logits[invalid_token_mask] = -float('inf')
306	        return logits
307	
308	
309	class LogitBiasLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):
310	
311	    def __init__(self, pin_memory: bool, device: torch.device):
312	        super().__init__()
313	        self.biases: dict[int, dict[int, float]] = {}
314	        self.device = device
315	        self.pin_memory = pin_memory
316	
317	        self.bias_tensor: torch.Tensor = torch.tensor(())
318	        self.logits_slice = (self._device_tensor([], torch.int32),
319	                             self._device_tensor([], torch.int32))
320	
321	    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
322	        """Logit bias can rebalance token probabilities and change the
323	        outcome of argmax in greedy sampling."""
324	        return False
325	
326	    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
327	        if not batch_update:
328	            return
329	
330	        # Process added requests.
331	        needs_update = bool(batch_update.added)
332	        for index, params, _ in batch_update.added:
333	            if isinstance(params, SamplingParams) and (lb :=
334	                                                       params.logit_bias):
335	                self.biases[index] = lb
336	            else:
337	                self.biases.pop(index, None)
338	
339	        if self.biases:
340	            # Process removed requests.
341	            for index in batch_update.removed:
342	                if self.biases.pop(index, None):
343	                    needs_update = True
344	
345	            # Process moved requests, unidirectional (a->b) and swap (a<->b)
346	            for a_index, b_index, direct in batch_update.moved:
347	                if direct == MoveDirectionality.UNIDIRECTIONAL:
348	                    if (a_entry := self.biases.pop(a_index, None)) is None:
349	                        if self.biases.pop(b_index, None) is not None:
350	                            needs_update = True
351	                    else:
352	                        self.biases[b_index] = a_entry
353	                        needs_update = True
354	                else:
355	                    a_entry = self.biases.pop(a_index, None)
356	                    if (b_entry := self.biases.pop(b_index, None)) is not None:
357	                        self.biases[a_index] = b_entry
358	                        needs_update = True
359	                    if a_entry is not None:
360	                        self.biases[b_index] = a_entry
361	                        needs_update = True
362	
363	        # Update tensors if needed.
364	        if needs_update:
365	            reqs, tok_ids, biases = [], [], []
366	            for req, lb in self.biases.items():
367	                reqs.extend([req] * len(lb))
368	                tok_ids.extend(lb.keys())
369	                biases.extend(lb.values())
370	
371	            self.bias_tensor = self._device_tensor(biases, torch.float32)
372	            self.logits_slice = (self._device_tensor(reqs, torch.int32),
373	                                 self._device_tensor(tok_ids, torch.int32))
374	
375	    def _device_tensor(self, data: list, dtype: torch.dtype) -> torch.Tensor:
376	        return (torch.tensor(data,
377	                             device="cpu",
378	                             dtype=dtype,
379	                             pin_memory=self.pin_memory).to(device=self.device,
380	                                                            non_blocking=True))
381	
382	    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
383	        if self.biases:
384	            logits[self.logits_slice] += self.bias_tensor
385	        return logits
386	
387	
388	class MinTokensLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):
389	
390	    def __init__(self, pin_memory: bool, device: torch.device):
391	        # index -> (min_toks, output_token_ids, stop_token_ids)
392	        super().__init__()
393	        self.min_toks: dict[int, tuple[int, Sequence[int], set[int]]] = {}
394	        self.device = device
395	        self.pin_memory = pin_memory
396	
397	        # (req_idx_tensor,eos_tok_id_tensor)
398	        self.logits_slice: tuple[torch.Tensor,
399	                                 torch.Tensor] = (self._device_tensor(
400	                                     [], torch.int32),
401	                                                  self._device_tensor(
402	                                                      [], torch.int32))
403	
404	    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
405	        """By censoring stop tokens, min-tokens can change the outcome
406	        of the argmax operation in greedy sampling."""
407	        return False
408	
409	    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
410	        needs_update = False
411	
412	        if batch_update:
413	            # Process added requests.
414	            needs_update |= bool(batch_update.added)
415	            for index, params, output_tok_ids in batch_update.added:
416	                if (isinstance(params, SamplingParams)
417	                        and (min_tokens := params.min_tokens)
418	                        and len(output_tok_ids) < min_tokens):
419	                    # Replace request metadata at batch index
420	                    self.min_toks[index] = (min_tokens, output_tok_ids,
421	                                            params.all_stop_token_ids)
422	                else:
423	                    # Drop request metadata at batch index
424	                    self.min_toks.pop(index, None)
425	
426	            if self.min_toks:
427	                # Process removed requests.
428	                for index in batch_update.removed:
429	                    if self.min_toks.pop(index, None):
430	                        needs_update = True
431	
432	                # Process moved requests, unidirectional (a->b) and
433	                # swapped (a<->b)
434	                for a_index, b_index, direct in batch_update.moved:
435	                    if direct == MoveDirectionality.UNIDIRECTIONAL:
436	                        if (a_entry := self.min_toks.pop(a_index,
437	                                                         None)) is None:
438	                            if self.min_toks.pop(b_index, None) is not None:
439	                                needs_update = True
440	                        else:
441	                            self.min_toks[b_index] = a_entry
442	                            needs_update = True
443	                    else:
444	                        a_entry = self.min_toks.pop(a_index, None)
445	                        if (b_entry := self.min_toks.pop(b_index,
446	                                                         None)) is not None:
447	                            self.min_toks[a_index] = b_entry
448	                            needs_update = True
449	                        if a_entry is not None:
450	                            self.min_toks[b_index] = a_entry
451	                            needs_update = True
452	
453	        if self.min_toks:
454	            # Check for any requests that have attained their min tokens.
455	            to_remove = tuple(index for index, (min_toks, out_tok_ids,
456	                                                _) in self.min_toks.items()
457	                              if len(out_tok_ids) >= min_toks)
458	            if to_remove:
459	                needs_update = True
460	                for index in to_remove:
461	                    del self.min_toks[index]
462	
463	        # Update tensors if needed.
464	        if needs_update:
465	            reqs: list[int] = []
466	            tok_ids: list[int] = []
467	            for req, (_, _, stop_tok_ids) in self.min_toks.items():
468	                reqs.extend([req] * len(stop_tok_ids))
469	                tok_ids.extend(stop_tok_ids)
470	
471	            self.logits_slice = (self._device_tensor(reqs, torch.int32),
472	                                 self._device_tensor(tok_ids, torch.int32))
473	
474	    def _device_tensor(self, data: list, dtype: torch.dtype) -> torch.Tensor:
475	        return (torch.tensor(data,
476	                             device="cpu",
477	                             dtype=dtype,
478	                             pin_memory=self.pin_memory).to(device=self.device,
479	                                                            non_blocking=True))
480	
481	    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
482	        if self.min_toks:
483	            # Inhibit EOS token for requests which have not reached min length
484	            logits[self.logits_slice] = -float("inf")
485	        return logits
486	
487	
488	def init_builtin_logitsprocs(pin_memory_available: bool, max_num_reqs: int,
489	                             device: torch.device) -> LogitsProcessorManager:
490	    """Construct 'builtin' vLLM logitsprocs which the engine
491	    loads by default.
492	
493	    Args:
494	      pin_memory_available: pinned memory is available for use
495	                            for use by logitsproc
496	      max_num_reqs: ceiling on request count in persistent batch
497	      device: inference device
498	
499	    Returns:
500	      Data structure encapsulating loaded logitsprocs
501	    """
502	    min_tokens_logitproc = MinTokensLogitsProcessor(
503	        pin_memory=pin_memory_available, device=device)
504	    logit_bias_logitproc = LogitBiasLogitsProcessor(
505	        pin_memory=pin_memory_available, device=device)
506	    min_p_logitproc = MinPLogitsProcessor(
507	        pin_memory=pin_memory_available,
508	        device=device,
509	        # +1 for temporary swap space
510	        max_num_reqs=max_num_reqs + 1)
511	    return LogitsProcessorManager(
512	        non_argmax_invariant=[
513	            min_tokens_logitproc,
514	            logit_bias_logitproc,
515	        ],
516	        argmax_invariant=[min_p_logitproc],
517	    )

128	    async def create_chat_completion(
129	        self,
130	        request: ChatCompletionRequest,
131	        raw_request: Optional[Request] = None,
132	    ) -> Union[AsyncGenerator[str, None], ChatCompletionResponse,
133	               ErrorResponse]:
134	        """
135	        Chat Completion API similar to OpenAI's API.
136	
137	        See https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create
138	        for the API specification. This API mimics the OpenAI
139	        Chat Completion API.
140	        """
141	        error_check_ret = await self._check_model(request)
142	        if error_check_ret is not None:
143	            logger.error("Error with model %s", error_check_ret)
144	            return error_check_ret
145	
146	        # If the engine is dead, raise the engine's DEAD_ERROR.
147	        # This is required for the streaming case, where we return a
148	        # success status before we actually start generating text :).
149	        if self.engine_client.errored:
150	            raise self.engine_client.dead_error
151	
152	        try:
153	            (
154	                lora_request,
155	                prompt_adapter_request,
156	            ) = self._maybe_get_adapters(request)
157	
158	            model_name = self._get_model_name(request.model, lora_request)
159	
160	            tokenizer = await self.engine_client.get_tokenizer(lora_request)
161	
162	            tool_parser = self.tool_parser
163	
164	            if isinstance(tokenizer, MistralTokenizer):
165	                # because of issues with pydantic we need to potentially
166	                # re-serialize the tool_calls field of the request
167	                # for more info: see comment in `maybe_serialize_tool_calls`
168	                maybe_serialize_tool_calls(request)
169	                truncate_tool_call_ids(request)
170	                validate_request_params(request)
171	
172	            if (request.tool_choice == "auto" and
173	                    not (self.enable_auto_tools and tool_parser is not None)
174	                    and not isinstance(tokenizer, MistralTokenizer)):
175	                # for hf tokenizers, "auto" tools requires
176	                # --enable-auto-tool-choice and --tool-call-parser
177	                return self.create_error_response(
178	                    "\"auto\" tool choice requires "
179	                    "--enable-auto-tool-choice and --tool-call-parser to be set"
180	                )
181	
182	            if request.tools is None:
183	                tool_dicts = None
184	            elif (request.tool_choice == "none"
185	                  and not self.expand_tools_even_if_tool_choice_none):
186	                if len(request.tools) > 0:
187	                    logger.warning_once(
188	                        "Tools are specified but tool_choice is set to 'none' "
189	                        "and --expand-tools-even-if-tool-choice-none is not "
190	                        "enabled. Tool definitions will be excluded from the "
191	                        "prompt. This behavior will change in vLLM v0.10 where "
192	                        "tool definitions will be included by default even "
193	                        "with tool_choice='none'. To adopt the new behavior "
194	                        "now, use --expand-tools-even-if-tool-choice-none. "
195	                        "To suppress this warning, either remove tools from "
196	                        "the request or set tool_choice to a different value.")
197	                tool_dicts = None
198	            else:
199	                tool_dicts = [tool.model_dump() for tool in request.tools]
200	
201	            (
202	                conversation,
203	                request_prompts,
204	                engine_prompts,
205	            ) = await self._preprocess_chat(
206	                request,
207	                tokenizer,
208	                request.messages,
209	                chat_template=request.chat_template or self.chat_template,
210	                chat_template_content_format=self.chat_template_content_format,
211	                add_generation_prompt=request.add_generation_prompt,
212	                continue_final_message=request.continue_final_message,
213	                tool_dicts=tool_dicts,
214	                documents=request.documents,
215	                chat_template_kwargs=request.chat_template_kwargs,
216	                tool_parser=tool_parser,
217	                truncate_prompt_tokens=request.truncate_prompt_tokens,
218	                add_special_tokens=request.add_special_tokens,
219	            )
220	        except (ValueError, TypeError, RuntimeError,
221	                jinja2.TemplateError) as e:
222	            logger.exception("Error in preprocessing prompt inputs")
223	            return self.create_error_response(f"{e} {e.__cause__}")
224	
225	        request_id = "chatcmpl-" \
226	                     f"{self._base_request_id(raw_request, request.request_id)}"
227	
228	        request_metadata = RequestResponseMetadata(request_id=request_id)
229	        if raw_request:
230	            raw_request.state.request_metadata = request_metadata
231	
232	        # Schedule the request and get the result generator.
233	        generators: list[AsyncGenerator[RequestOutput, None]] = []
234	        try:
235	            for i, engine_prompt in enumerate(engine_prompts):
236	                sampling_params: Union[SamplingParams, BeamSearchParams]
237	
238	                if self.default_sampling_params is None:
239	                    self.default_sampling_params = {}

241	                max_tokens = get_max_tokens(

255	                self._log_inputs(request_id,

278	                        prompt_adapter_request=prompt_adapter_request,
279	                        priority=request.priority,

388	        cls,

390	        vocab_size: int,
391	        device: torch.device,
392	        dtype: torch.dtype,
393	    ) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool]:
394	        prompt_tokens: list[array] = []
395	        output_tokens: list[array] = []
396	        top_ks: list[int] = []
397	        temperatures: list[float] = []
398	        top_ps: list[float] = []
399	        min_ps: list[float] = []
400	        presence_penalties: list[float] = []
401	        frequency_penalties: list[float] = []
402	        repetition_penalties: list[float] = []
403	        do_penalties = False
404	        do_top_p_top_k = False
405	        do_min_p = False
406	
407	        assert sampling_metadata.seq_groups is not None
408	        for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
409	            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
410	            sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
411	            temperature = sampling_params.temperature
412	            p = sampling_params.presence_penalty
413	            f = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
414	            r = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
415	            top_p = sampling_params.top_p
416	            min_p = sampling_params.min_p
417	
418	            # k should not be greater than the vocab size.
419	            top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
420	            top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
421	            if temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
422	                # NOTE: Zero temperature means deterministic sampling
423	                # (i.e., greedy sampling or beam search).
424	                # Set the temperature to 1 to avoid division by zero.
425	                temperature = 1.0
426	            if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
427	                                       or top_k != vocab_size):
428	                do_top_p_top_k = True
429	            if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
430	                do_min_p = True
431	            if not do_penalties and (abs(p) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
432	                                     or abs(f) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
433	                                     or abs(r - 1.0) >= _SAMPLING_EPS):
434	                do_penalties = True
435	
436	            is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
437	            if is_prompt and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
438	                # For tokens in the prompt that we only need to get
439	                # their logprobs
440	                query_len = seq_group.query_len
441	                assert query_len is not None
442	                prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
443	                temperatures += [temperature] * prefill_len
444	                top_ps += [top_p] * prefill_len
445	                top_ks += [top_k] * prefill_len
446	                min_ps += [min_p] * prefill_len
447	                presence_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
448	                frequency_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
449	                repetition_penalties += [1] * prefill_len
450	
451	            if seq_group.do_sample:
452	                sample_lens = len(seq_group.sample_indices)
453	                assert sample_lens >= len(seq_ids)
454	                temperatures += [temperature] * sample_lens
455	                top_ps += [top_p] * sample_lens
456	                top_ks += [top_k] * sample_lens
457	                min_ps += [min_p] * sample_lens
458	                presence_penalties += [p] * sample_lens
459	                frequency_penalties += [f] * sample_lens
460	                repetition_penalties += [r] * sample_lens
461	
462	        if do_penalties:
463	            for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
464	                seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
465	                sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
466	                if (seq_group.is_prompt
467	                        and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None):
468	                    prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
469	                    prompt_tokens.extend(
470	                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
471	                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
472	                    output_tokens.extend(
473	                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
474	                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
475	                if seq_group.do_sample:
476	                    for seq_id in seq_ids:
477	                        seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
478	                        prompt_tokens.append(seq_data.prompt_token_ids_array)
479	                        output_tokens.append(seq_data.output_token_ids_array)
480	
481	        sampling_tensors = SamplingTensors.from_lists(
482	            temperatures,
483	            top_ps,
484	            top_ks,
485	            min_ps,
486	            presence_penalties,
487	            frequency_penalties,
488	            repetition_penalties,
489	            prompt_tokens,
490	            output_tokens,
491	            vocab_size,
492	            device,
493	            dtype,
494	        )
495	        return (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p)

29	        # NOTE(woosuk): Use the original logits (before any penalties or

32	        # is used for sampling (after penalties and temperature scaling).

39	        # Use float32 for the logits.

43	        # Apply bad words exclusion.
44	        logits = self.apply_bad_words(logits, sampling_metadata)
45	
46	        # Apply logits processors which can impact greedy sampling
47	        for processor in (sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.non_argmax_invariant):
48	            logits = processor.apply(logits)
49	
50	        # Apply penalties (e.g., min_tokens, freq_penalties).
51	        logits = self.apply_penalties(logits, sampling_metadata)
52	        # Sample the next token.
53	        sampled = self.sample(logits, sampling_metadata)
54	        # Convert sampled token ids to int64 (long) type to ensure compatibility
55	        # with subsequent operations that may use these values as indices.
56	        # This conversion is necessary because FlashInfer sampling operations
57	        # return int32 (while PyTorch argmax and topk return int64).
58	        sampled = sampled.long()
59	
60	        # Gather the logprobs of the topk and sampled token (if requested).
61	        # Get logprobs and rank tensors (if requested)
62	        logprobs_tensors = None if num_logprobs is None else \
63	            self.gather_logprobs(raw_logprobs, num_logprobs, token_ids=sampled)
64	
65	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
66	        sampled = sampled.to(torch.int32)

71	            # [num_requests, 1], where each row represents one generated

20	class MoveDirectionality(Enum):
21	    # One-way i1->i2 req move within batch
22	    UNIDIRECTIONAL = 0
23	    # Two-way i1<->i2 req swap within batch
24	    SWAP = 1
25	
26	
27	# (index, params, output_tok_ids) tuples for new
28	# requests added to the batch.
29	AddedRequest = tuple[int, Union[SamplingParams, PoolingParams], list[int]]
30	# (index 1, index 2, directionality) tuples representing
31	# one-way moves or two-way swaps of requests in batch
32	MovedRequest = tuple[int, int, MoveDirectionality]
33	# Batch indices of any removed requests.
34	RemovedRequest = int
35	
36	
37	@dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)
38	class BatchUpdate:
39	    """Persistent batch state change info for logitsprocs"""
40	    batch_size: int  # Current num reqs in batch
41	
42	    # Metadata for requests added to, removed from, and moved
43	    # within the persistent batch.
44	    #
45	    # Note: each added request is represented as
46	    # (index, params, output_tok_ids)
47	    # Key assumption: output_tok_ids is a reference to the
48	    # request's running output tokens list; in this way
49	    # the logits processors always see the latest list of
50	    # generated tokens
51	    removed: Sequence[RemovedRequest]
52	    moved: Sequence[MovedRequest]
53	    added: Sequence[AddedRequest]
54	
55	
56	class BatchUpdateBuilder:
57	    """Helps track persistent batch state changes and build
58	    a batch update data structure for logitsprocs
59	    
60	    Assumptions:
61	    * All information about requests removed from persistent batch
62	      during a step is aggregated in self._removed through calls to
63	      self.removed_append() at the beginning of a step. This must happen
64	      before the first time that self.removed, self.pop_removed()
65	      or self.peek_removed() are invoked in a given step
66	    * After the first time that self.removed, self.pop_removed()
67	      or self.peek_removed() are read in a step, no new removals
68	      are registered using self.removed_append()
69	    * Elements of self._removed are never directly modified, added or
70	      removed (i.e. modification is only via self.removed_append() and
71	      self.pop_removed())
72	    
73	    Guarantees under above assumptions:
74	    * self.removed is always sorted in descending order
75	    * self.pop_removed() and self.peek_removed() both return
76	      the lowest removed request index in the current step
77	    """
78	
79	    _removed: list[RemovedRequest]
80	    _is_removed_sorted: bool
81	    moved: list[MovedRequest]
82	    added: list[AddedRequest]
83	
84	    def __init__(
85	        self,
86	        removed: Optional[list[RemovedRequest]] = None,
87	        moved: Optional[list[MovedRequest]] = None,
88	        added: Optional[list[AddedRequest]] = None,
89	    ) -> None:
90	        self._removed = removed or []
91	        self.moved = moved or []
92	        self.added = added or []
93	        self._is_removed_sorted = False
94	
95	    def _ensure_removed_sorted(self) -> None:
96	        """Sort removed request indices in
97	        descending order.
98	        
99	        Idempotent after first call in a
100	        given step, until reset.
101	        """

11	
12	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5
13	
14	
15	class Sampler(nn.Module):
16	
17	    def __init__(self):
18	        super().__init__()
19	        self.topk_topp_sampler = TopKTopPSampler()
20	
21	    def forward(
22	        self,
23	        logits: torch.Tensor,
24	        sampling_metadata: TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata,
25	    ) -> SamplerOutput:
26	        # Use float32 for the logits.
27	        logits = logits.to(torch.float32)
28	        # Sample the next token.
29	        sampled = self.sample(logits, sampling_metadata)
30	
31	        # These are TPU tensors.
32	        sampler_output = SamplerOutput(
33	            # The sampled tokens are expanded to 2D tensor with shape
34	            # [num_requests, 1], where each row represents one generated
35	            # token per request.
36	            sampled_token_ids=sampled.unsqueeze(-1),
37	            logprobs_tensors=None)
38	        return sampler_output
39	
40	    def apply_temperature(
41	        self,
42	        logits: torch.Tensor,
43	        temp: torch.Tensor,
44	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
45	        return logits.div_(temp.unsqueeze(dim=1))
46	
47	    def greedy_sample(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
48	        return logits.argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
49	
50	    def sample(
51	        self,
52	        logits: torch.Tensor,
53	        sampling_metadata: TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata,
54	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
55	        greedy_sampled = self.greedy_sample(logits)
56	
57	        assert sampling_metadata.temperature is not None
58	
59	        # Apply temperature.
60	        logits = self.apply_temperature(logits, sampling_metadata.temperature)
61	
62	        # Apply min_p.
63	        if sampling_metadata.min_p is not None:
64	            logits = self.apply_min_p(logits, sampling_metadata.min_p)
65	
66	        # Apply top_k and/or top_p.
67	        random_sampled = self.topk_topp_sampler(
68	            logits,
69	            sampling_metadata.generators,
70	            sampling_metadata.top_k,
71	            sampling_metadata.top_p,
72	        )
73	
74	        sampled = torch.where(sampling_metadata.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS,
75	                              greedy_sampled, random_sampled)
76	        return sampled
77	
78	    def compute_logprobs(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
79	        return logits.log_softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
80	
81	    def gather_logprobs(
82	        self,
83	        logprobs: torch.Tensor,
84	        num_logprobs: int,
85	        token_ids: torch.Tensor,
86	    ) -> LogprobsTensors:
87	        """
88	        Gather logprobs for topk and sampled/prompt token.
89	
90	        Args:
91	          logits: (num tokens) x (vocab) tensor
92	          num_logprobs: minimum number of logprobs to
93	                        retain per token
94	          token_ids: prompt tokens (if prompt logprobs)
95	                     or sampled tokens (if sampled
96	                     logprobs); 1D token ID tensor
97	                     with (num tokens) elements
98	
99	        Returns:
100	          Top-k int indices tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
101	          Top-k float logprobs tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
102	          Sampled token rank tensor, (num tokens)
103	        """
104	        # Find the topK values.
105	        topk_logprobs, topk_indices = torch.topk(logprobs,
106	                                                 num_logprobs,
107	                                                 dim=-1)
108	
109	        # Get with the logprob of the prompt or sampled token.
110	        token_ids = token_ids.unsqueeze(-1)
111	        token_logprobs = logprobs.gather(-1, token_ids)
112	
113	        # Compute the ranks of the actual token.
114	        token_ranks = (logprobs >= token_logprobs).sum(-1)
115	
116	        # Concatenate together with the topk.
117	        indices = torch.cat((token_ids, topk_indices), dim=1)
118	        logprobs = torch.cat((token_logprobs, topk_logprobs), dim=1)
119	
120	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
121	        indices = indices.to(torch.int32)
122	
123	        return LogprobsTensors(indices, logprobs, token_ranks)
124	
125	    def apply_min_p(
126	        self,
127	        logits: torch.Tensor,
128	        min_p: torch.Tensor,
129	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
130	        """
131	        Filters logits using adaptive probability thresholding.
132	        """
133	        # Convert logits to probability distribution
134	        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
135	        # Calculate maximum probabilities per sequence
136	        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values,
137	                                       dim=-1,
138	                                       keepdim=True)
139	        # Reshape min_p for broadcasting
140	        adjusted_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze(1) * max_probabilities
141	        # Identify valid tokens using threshold comparison
142	        valid_token_mask = probability_values >= adjusted_min_p
143	        # Apply mask using boolean indexing (xla friendly)
144	        logits.masked_fill_(~valid_token_mask, -float("inf"))
145	        return logits

11	    Callable[[list[int], torch.Tensor], torch.Tensor],

13	]
14	"""LogitsProcessor is a function that takes a list
15	of previously generated tokens, the logits tensor
16	for the next token and, optionally, prompt tokens as a
17	first argument, and returns a modified tensor of logits
18	to sample from."""
19	
20	
21	def get_bad_words_logits_processors(
22	        bad_words: list[str],
23	        tokenizer: AnyTokenizer) -> list[LogitsProcessor]:
24	    bad_words_ids: list[list[int]] = list()
25	
26	    for bad_word in bad_words:
27	        # To prohibit words both at the beginning
28	        # and in the middle of text
29	        # (related to add_prefix_space tokenizer parameter)
30	        for add_prefix_space in [False, True]:
31	            prefix = " " if add_prefix_space else ""
32	            prompt = prefix + bad_word.lstrip()
33	
34	            prompt_token_ids = tokenizer.encode(text=prompt,
35	                                                add_special_tokens=False)
36	
37	            # If no space at the beginning
38	            # or if prefix space produces a new word token
39	            if (not add_prefix_space) or (
40	                    add_prefix_space
41	                    and prompt_token_ids[0] != bad_words_ids[-1][0]
42	                    and len(prompt_token_ids) == len(bad_words_ids[-1])):
43	                bad_words_ids.append(prompt_token_ids)
44	
45	    return [NoBadWordsLogitsProcessor(bad_words_ids=bad_words_ids)]
46	
47	
48	class NoBadWordsLogitsProcessor:
49	    _SMALLEST_LOGIT = float("-inf")
50	    _NEUTRAL_LOGIT = 0.0
51	
52	    def __init__(self, bad_words_ids: list[list[int]]):
53	        self.bad_words_ids = bad_words_ids
54	        self.word_bias: torch.FloatTensor = None
55	
56	    def __call__(
57	        self,
58	        past_tokens_ids: Union[list[int], tuple[int]],
59	        logits: torch.FloatTensor,
60	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
61	        if self.word_bias is None:
62	            self._init_word_bias(logits=logits)
63	
64	        last_token_bias = torch.zeros_like(logits)
65	
66	        for bad_word_ids in self.bad_words_ids:
67	            if len(bad_word_ids) == 1:  # 1-token words already processed
68	                continue
69	
70	            if len(bad_word_ids) > len(past_tokens_ids) + 1:
71	                continue
72	
73	            prefix_length = len(bad_word_ids) - 1
74	            last_token_id = bad_word_ids[-1]
75	            actual_prefix = past_tokens_ids[-prefix_length:]
76	            expected_prefix = bad_word_ids[:prefix_length]
77	
78	            assert len(actual_prefix) == len(expected_prefix)
79	
80	            is_match = tuple(actual_prefix) == tuple(expected_prefix)
81	            last_token_bias[last_token_id] += (self._SMALLEST_LOGIT if is_match
82	                                               else self._NEUTRAL_LOGIT)
83	
84	        logits = logits + self.word_bias + last_token_bias
85	
86	        return logits
87	
88	    def _init_word_bias(self, logits: torch.FloatTensor) -> None:
89	        # Code based on NoBadWordsLogitsProcessor and SequenceBiasLogitsProcessor  # noqa: E501
90	        # from https://github.com/huggingface/transformers/blob/main/src/transformers/generation/logits_process.py
91	
92	        vocab_size = logits.shape[-1]
93	
94	        self._check_token_ids_bounds(vocab_size=vocab_size)
95	
96	        self.word_bias = torch.zeros((vocab_size, ),
97	                                     dtype=torch.float,
98	                                     device=logits.device)
99	
100	        for bad_word_ids in self.bad_words_ids:
101	            if len(bad_word_ids) == 1:
102	                bad_word_id = bad_word_ids[-1]
103	                self.word_bias[bad_word_id] = self._SMALLEST_LOGIT
104	
105	    def _check_token_ids_bounds(self, vocab_size: int) -> None:
106	        invalid_token_ids = []
107	
108	        for bad_word_ids in self.bad_words_ids:
109	            for token_id in bad_word_ids:
110	                if token_id < 0 or token_id >= vocab_size:
111	                    invalid_token_ids.append(token_id)
112	
113	        if len(invalid_token_ids) > 0:
114	            raise ValueError(
115	                f"The model vocabulary size is {vocab_size},"
116	                f" but the following tokens"
117	                f" were specified as bad: {invalid_token_ids}."
118	                f" All token id values should be integers satisfying:"
119	                f" 0 <= token_id < {vocab_size}.")

68

70	        self,
71	        logits: torch.Tensor,

121	def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
122	    logits: torch.Tensor,
123	    k: torch.Tensor,
124	    p: torch.Tensor,
125	) -> torch.Tensor:
126	    """
127	    Apply top-k and top-p optimized for TPU.
128	
129	    This algorithm avoids using torch.scatter which is extremely slow on TPU.
130	    This is achieved by finding a "cut-off" element in the original logit, and
131	    after thresholding the logit using this cut-off, the remaining elements
132	    shall constitute the top-p set.
133	
134	    Note: in the case of tie (i.e. multipple cut-off elements present in the
135	    logit), all tie elements are included in the top-p set. In other words,
136	    this function does not break ties. Instead, these tie tokens have equal
137	    chance of being chosen during final sampling, so we can consider the tie
138	    being broken then.
139	    """
140	    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
141	    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
142	
143	    if k is not None:
144	        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: (batch, )
145	        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
146	        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
147	
148	        # Make sure the no top-k rows are no-op.
149	        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
150	        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
151	
152	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_k_cutoff
153	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
154	
155	    if p is not None:
156	        cumprob = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
157	        top_p_mask = cumprob <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
158	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # at least one
159	
160	        top_p_count = top_p_mask.sum(dim=-1).unsqueeze(1)
161	        top_p_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_p_count)
162	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_p_cutoff
163	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
164	
165	    return logits
166	
167	
168	def apply_top_k_top_p(
169	    logits: torch.Tensor,
170	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
171	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
172	) -> torch.Tensor:
173	    """Apply top-k and top-p masks to the logits.
174	
175	    If a top-p is used, this function will sort the logits tensor,
176	    which can be slow for large batches.
177	
178	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
179	    """

768	def _get_ranks(x: torch.Tensor, indices: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
769	    """
770	    This function calculates the ranks of the chosen tokens in a logprob tensor.
771	
772	    Args:
773	        x (torch.Tensor): 2D logprob tensor of shape (N, M)
774	                        where N is the no. of tokens and M is the vocab dim.
775	        indices (torch.Tensor): List of chosen token indices.
776	
777	    Returns:
778	        torch.Tensor: 1D tensor of shape (N,) where N is the no. of tokens.
779	                    Each element in the returned tensor represents the rank
780	                    of the chosen token in the input logprob tensor.
781	    """
782	    vals = x[torch.arange(0, len(x), device=x.device, dtype=indices.dtype),
783	             indices]
784	    result = (x > vals[:, None])
785	    del vals
786	    return result.sum(1).add_(1)

794	    """Return sample logprobs and prompt logprobs.

806	            seq groups, it is sorted by prefill tokens for seq_group_1 (if
807	            prompt logprob is enabled), decode tokens for seq_group_1 (if
808	            sampling is required), prefill tokens for seq_group_2, ...
809	        sampling_metadata: The sampling metadata.

812	            sample_results can contain different number of seq_ids from
813	            sampling_metadata.seq_groups. It is because beam search creates
814	            2 * BEAM_WIDTH number of samples (whereas there are only up to

830	    # Select indices to compute logprob from, ranks of token ids, and the top
831	    # k token ids from logprobs.
832	    for (seq_group, sample_result) in zip(sampling_metadata.seq_groups,
833	                                          sample_results):

835	
836	        # Update indices and tokens for prompt logprobs.
837	        if (seq_group.is_prompt
838	                and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None):
839	            largest_num_logprobs = max(largest_num_logprobs,
840	                                       sampling_params.prompt_logprobs)
841	            next_prompt_tokens = _get_next_prompt_tokens(seq_group)
842	            query_indices.extend(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
843	            next_token_ids.extend(next_prompt_tokens)
844	
845	        # Update indices and next tokenes for sample logprob.
846	        if seq_group.do_sample:
847	            token_ids, parent_seq_ids = sample_result
848	            # NOTE: We cannot directly use sample_indices because
849	            # sample_indices only contain parent seq_ids of a previous step.

417:        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
418:            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
420:        if self.top_k < -1:
421:            raise ValueError(f"top_k must be 0 (disable), or at least 1, "
422:                             f"got {self.top_k}.")
423:        if not isinstance(self.top_k, int):
425:                f"top_k must be an integer, got {type(self.top_k).__name__}")
426:        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
427:            raise ValueError("min_p must be in [0, 1], got "
428:                             f"{self.min_p}.")
570:            f"top_p={self.top_p}, "
571:            f"top_k={self.top_k}, "
572:            f"min_p={self.min_p}, "

214	        default_factory=list)  # non-argmax-invariant logitsprocs

224	
225	class MinPLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):
226	
227	    def __init__(self, max_num_reqs: int, pin_memory: bool,
228	                 device: DeviceLikeType):
229	        super().__init__()
230	        self.min_p_count: int = 0
231	
232	        self.min_p_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros((max_num_reqs, ),
233	                                            dtype=torch.float32,
234	                                            device="cpu",
235	                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
236	        self.min_p_cpu = self.min_p_cpu_tensor.numpy()
237	        # Pre-allocated device tensor
238	        self.min_p_device: torch.Tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
239	                                                      dtype=torch.float32,
240	                                                      device=device)
241	        # Current slice of the device tensor
242	        self.min_p: torch.Tensor = self.min_p_device[:0]
243	
244	    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
245	        """Min-p never impacts greedy sampling"""
246	        return True
247	
248	    def get_min_p_by_index(self, index: int) -> float:
249	        return float(self.min_p_cpu[index])
250	
251	    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
252	        if not batch_update:
253	            return
254	
255	        needs_update = False
256	        # Process added requests.
257	        for index, params, _ in batch_update.added:
258	            min_p = params.min_p if isinstance(params, SamplingParams) else 0.0
259	            if self.min_p_cpu[index] != min_p:
260	                needs_update = True
261	                self.min_p_cpu[index] = min_p
262	            if min_p:
263	                self.min_p_count += 1
264	
265	        if self.min_p_count:
266	            # Process removed requests.
267	            needs_update |= bool(batch_update.removed)
268	            for index in batch_update.removed:
269	                if self.min_p_cpu[index]:
270	                    self.min_p_count -= 1
271	
272	            # Process moved requests, unidirectional (a->b) and swap (a<->b)
273	            for adx, bdx, direct in batch_update.moved:
274	                change = (min_p_a :=
275	                          self.min_p_cpu[adx]) != (min_p_b :=
276	                                                   self.min_p_cpu[bdx])
277	                needs_update |= change
278	                if change:
279	                    self.min_p_cpu[bdx] = min_p_a
280	                    if direct == MoveDirectionality.SWAP:
281	                        self.min_p_cpu[adx] = min_p_b
282	
283	        # Update tensors if needed.
284	        size = batch_update.batch_size
285	        if self.min_p_count and (needs_update or self.min_p.shape[0] != size):
286	            self.min_p = self.min_p_device[:size]
287	            self.min_p.copy_(self.min_p_cpu_tensor[:size], non_blocking=True)
288	            self.min_p.unsqueeze_(1)
289	
290	    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
291	        if not self.min_p_count:
292	            return logits
293	
294	        # Convert logits to probability distribution
295	        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
296	        # Calculate maximum probabilities per sequence
297	        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values,
298	                                       dim=-1,
299	                                       keepdim=True)
300	        # Adjust min_p
301	        adjusted_min_p = max_probabilities.mul_(self.min_p)
302	        # Identify valid tokens using threshold comparison
303	        invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_min_p
304	        # Apply mask using boolean indexing
305	        logits[invalid_token_mask] = -float('inf')
306	        return logits

3	"""Sampler layer implementing TPU supported operations."""
4	
5	import torch
6	import torch.nn as nn
7	
8	from vllm.v1.outputs import LogprobsTensors, SamplerOutput
9	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.topk_topp_sampler import TopKTopPSampler
10	from vllm.v1.sample.tpu.metadata import TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata
11	
12	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5
13	
14	
15	class Sampler(nn.Module):
16	
17	    def __init__(self):
18	        super().__init__()
19	        self.topk_topp_sampler = TopKTopPSampler()
20	
21	    def forward(
22	        self,
23	        logits: torch.Tensor,
24	        sampling_metadata: TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata,
25	    ) -> SamplerOutput:
26	        # Use float32 for the logits.
27	        logits = logits.to(torch.float32)
28	        # Sample the next token.
29	        sampled = self.sample(logits, sampling_metadata)
30	
31	        # These are TPU tensors.
32	        sampler_output = SamplerOutput(
33	            # The sampled tokens are expanded to 2D tensor with shape
34	            # [num_requests, 1], where each row represents one generated
35	            # token per request.
36	            sampled_token_ids=sampled.unsqueeze(-1),
37	            logprobs_tensors=None)
38	        return sampler_output
39	
40	    def apply_temperature(
41	        self,
42	        logits: torch.Tensor,
43	        temp: torch.Tensor,
44	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
45	        return logits.div_(temp.unsqueeze(dim=1))
46	
47	    def greedy_sample(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
48	        return logits.argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
49	
50	    def sample(
51	        self,
52	        logits: torch.Tensor,
53	        sampling_metadata: TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata,
54	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
55	        greedy_sampled = self.greedy_sample(logits)
56	
57	        assert sampling_metadata.temperature is not None
58	
59	        # Apply temperature.
60	        logits = self.apply_temperature(logits, sampling_metadata.temperature)
61	
62	        # Apply min_p.
63	        if sampling_metadata.min_p is not None:
64	            logits = self.apply_min_p(logits, sampling_metadata.min_p)
65	
66	        # Apply top_k and/or top_p.
67	        random_sampled = self.topk_topp_sampler(
68	            logits,
69	            sampling_metadata.generators,
70	            sampling_metadata.top_k,
71	            sampling_metadata.top_p,
72	        )
73	
74	        sampled = torch.where(sampling_metadata.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS,
75	                              greedy_sampled, random_sampled)
76	        return sampled
77	
78	    def compute_logprobs(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
79	        return logits.log_softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
80	
81	    def gather_logprobs(
82	        self,
83	        logprobs: torch.Tensor,
84	        num_logprobs: int,
85	        token_ids: torch.Tensor,
86	    ) -> LogprobsTensors:
87	        """
88	        Gather logprobs for topk and sampled/prompt token.
89	
90	        Args:
91	          logits: (num tokens) x (vocab) tensor
92	          num_logprobs: minimum number of logprobs to
93	                        retain per token
94	          token_ids: prompt tokens (if prompt logprobs)
95	                     or sampled tokens (if sampled
96	                     logprobs); 1D token ID tensor
97	                     with (num tokens) elements
98	
99	        Returns:
100	          Top-k int indices tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
