31	class GuidedDecodingParams:
32	    """One of these fields will be used to build a logit processor."""
33	    json: Optional[Union[str, dict]] = None
34	    regex: Optional[str] = None
35	    choice: Optional[list[str]] = None
36	    grammar: Optional[str] = None
37	    json_object: Optional[bool] = None
38	    """These are other options that can be set"""
39	    backend: Optional[str] = None
40	    backend_was_auto: bool = False
41	    disable_fallback: bool = False
42	    disable_any_whitespace: bool = False
43	    disable_additional_properties: bool = False
44	    whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None
45	    structural_tag: Optional[str] = None
46	
47	    @staticmethod
48	    def from_optional(
49	        json: Optional[Union[dict, BaseModel, str]] = None,
50	        regex: Optional[str] = None,
51	        choice: Optional[list[str]] = None,
52	        grammar: Optional[str] = None,
53	        json_object: Optional[bool] = None,
54	        backend: Optional[str] = None,
55	        whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None,
56	        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
57	    ) -> Optional["GuidedDecodingParams"]:
58	        if all(arg is None for arg in (json, regex, choice, grammar,
59	                                       json_object, structural_tag)):
60	            return None
61	        # Extract json schemas from pydantic models
62	        if isinstance(json, (BaseModel, type(BaseModel))):
63	            json = json.model_json_schema()
64	        return GuidedDecodingParams(
65	            json=json,
66	            regex=regex,
67	            choice=choice,
68	            grammar=grammar,
69	            json_object=json_object,
70	            backend=backend,
71	            whitespace_pattern=whitespace_pattern,
72	            structural_tag=structural_tag,
73	        )
74	
75	    def __post_init__(self):
76	        """Validate that some fields are mutually exclusive."""
77	        guide_count = sum([
78	            self.json is not None, self.regex is not None, self.choice
79	            is not None, self.grammar is not None, self.json_object is not None
80	        ])
81	        if guide_count > 1:
82	            raise ValueError(
83	                "You can only use one kind of guided decoding but multiple are "
84	                f"specified: {self.__dict__}")
85	
86	        if self.backend is not None and ":" in self.backend:
87	            self._extract_backend_options()
88	
89	    @deprecated(
90	        "Passing guided decoding backend options inside backend in the format "
91	        "'backend:...' is deprecated. This will be removed in v0.10.0. Please "
92	        "use the dedicated arguments '--disable-fallback', "
93	        "'--disable-any-whitespace' and '--disable-additional-properties' "
94	        "instead.")
95	    def _extract_backend_options(self):
96	        """Extract backend options from the backend string."""
97	        assert isinstance(self.backend, str)
98	        self.backend, options = self.backend.split(":")
99	        options_set = set(options.strip().split(","))
100	        if "no-fallback" in options_set:
101	            self.disable_fallback = True
102	        if "disable-any-whitespace" in options_set:
103	            self.disable_any_whitespace = True
104	        if "no-additional-properties" in options_set:
105	            self.disable_additional_properties = True

121	        dict=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
122	    """Sampling parameters for text generation.
123	
124	    Overall, we follow the sampling parameters from the OpenAI text completion
125	    API (https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/create).
126	    In addition, we support beam search, which is not supported by OpenAI.
127	
128	    Args:
129	        n: Number of output sequences to return for the given prompt.
130	        best_of: Number of output sequences that are generated from the prompt.
131	            From these `best_of` sequences, the top `n` sequences are returned.
132	            `best_of` must be greater than or equal to `n`. By default,
133	            `best_of` is set to `n`. Warning, this is only supported in V0.
134	        presence_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether they
135	            appear in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the model
136	            to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to repeat
137	            tokens.
138	        frequency_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on their
139	            frequency in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the
140	            model to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to
141	            repeat tokens.
142	        repetition_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether
143	            they appear in the prompt and the generated text so far. Values > 1
144	            encourage the model to use new tokens, while values < 1 encourage
145	            the model to repeat tokens.
146	        temperature: Float that controls the randomness of the sampling. Lower
147	            values make the model more deterministic, while higher values make
148	            the model more random. Zero means greedy sampling.
149	        top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens
150	            to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens.
151	        top_k: Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
152	            to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens.
153	        min_p: Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be
154	            considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token.
155	            Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this.
156	        seed: Random seed to use for the generation.
157	        stop: list of strings that stop the generation when they are generated.
158	            The returned output will not contain the stop strings.
159	        stop_token_ids: list of tokens that stop the generation when they are
160	            generated. The returned output will contain the stop tokens unless
161	            the stop tokens are special tokens.
162	        bad_words: list of words that are not allowed to be generated.
163	            More precisely, only the last token of a corresponding
164	            token sequence is not allowed when the next generated token
165	            can complete the sequence.
166	        include_stop_str_in_output: Whether to include the stop strings in
167	            output text. Defaults to False.
168	        ignore_eos: Whether to ignore the EOS token and continue generating
169	            tokens after the EOS token is generated.
170	        max_tokens: Maximum number of tokens to generate per output sequence.
171	        min_tokens: Minimum number of tokens to generate per output sequence
172	            before EOS or stop_token_ids can be generated
173	        logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per output token.
174	            When set to None, no probability is returned. If set to a non-None
175	            value, the result includes the log probabilities of the specified
176	            number of most likely tokens, as well as the chosen tokens.
177	            Note that the implementation follows the OpenAI API: The API will
178	            always return the log probability of the sampled token, so there

194	        logit_bias: If provided, the engine will construct a logits processor

196	        allowed_token_ids: If provided, the engine will construct a logits
197	            processor which only retains scores for the given token ids.
198	            Defaults to None.
199	        extra_args: Arbitrary additional args, that can be used by custom

73	    ) -> "TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata":
74	        """
75	        Copy sampling tensors slices from `input_batch` to on device tensors.
76	
77	        `InputBatch._make_sampling_metadata` causes recompilation on XLA as it 
78	        slices dynamic shapes on device tensors. This impl moves the dynamic 
79	        ops to CPU and produces tensors of fixed `padded_num_reqs` size.
80	
81	        Args:
82	            input_batch: The input batch containing sampling parameters.
83	            padded_num_reqs: The padded number of requests.
84	            xla_device: The XLA device.
85	            generate_params_if_all_greedy: If True, generate sampling parameters
86	                even if all requests are greedy. this is useful for cases where
87	                we want to pre-compile a graph with sampling parameters, even if
88	                they are not strictly needed for greedy decoding.
89	        """
90	        needs_logprobs = input_batch.max_num_logprobs>0 if \
91	            input_batch.max_num_logprobs else False
92	        # Early return to avoid unnecessary cpu to tpu copy
93	        if (input_batch.all_greedy is True
94	                and generate_params_if_all_greedy is False):
95	            return cls(all_greedy=True, logprobs=needs_logprobs)
96	
97	        num_reqs = input_batch.num_reqs
98	
99	        def fill_slice(cpu_tensor: torch.Tensor, fill_val) -> torch.Tensor:
100	            # Pad value is the default one.

100	          Top-k int indices tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
101	          Top-k float logprobs tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
102	          Sampled token rank tensor, (num tokens)
103	        """
104	        # Find the topK values.
105	        topk_logprobs, topk_indices = torch.topk(logprobs,
106	                                                 num_logprobs,
107	                                                 dim=-1)
108	
109	        # Get with the logprob of the prompt or sampled token.
110	        token_ids = token_ids.unsqueeze(-1)
111	        token_logprobs = logprobs.gather(-1, token_ids)
112	
113	        # Compute the ranks of the actual token.
114	        token_ranks = (logprobs >= token_logprobs).sum(-1)
115	
116	        # Concatenate together with the topk.
117	        indices = torch.cat((token_ids, topk_indices), dim=1)
118	        logprobs = torch.cat((token_logprobs, topk_logprobs), dim=1)
119	
120	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
121	        indices = indices.to(torch.int32)
122	
123	        return LogprobsTensors(indices, logprobs, token_ranks)

125	    def apply_min_p(
126	        self,
127	        logits: torch.Tensor,
128	        min_p: torch.Tensor,
129	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
130	        """
131	        Filters logits using adaptive probability thresholding.
132	        """
133	        # Convert logits to probability distribution
134	        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
135	        # Calculate maximum probabilities per sequence
136	        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values,
137	                                       dim=-1,
138	                                       keepdim=True)
139	        # Reshape min_p for broadcasting
140	        adjusted_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze(1) * max_probabilities
141	        # Identify valid tokens using threshold comparison
142	        valid_token_mask = probability_values >= adjusted_min_p
143	        # Apply mask using boolean indexing (xla friendly)
144	        logits.masked_fill_(~valid_token_mask, -float("inf"))
145	        return logits

600	    def _make_sampling_metadata(self) -> SamplingMetadata:

620	            copy_slice(self.repetition_penalties_cpu_tensor,

622

624	                                  (self.num_reqs > 0

626	        if needs_prompt_token_ids:

701	        """

703	        datastructures used to activate the current LoRAs.
704	        Returns:
705	            1. prompt_lora_mapping: A tuple of size self.num_reqs where,
706	               prompt_lora_mapping[i] is the LoRA id to use for the ith prompt.
707	            2. token_lora_mapping: A tuple of size np.sum(num_scheduled_tokens)
708	               where, token_lora_mapping[i] is the LoRA id to use for ith token.

712	        req_lora_mapping = self.request_lora_mapping[:self.num_reqs]

714	        token_lora_mapping = tuple(
715	            req_lora_mapping.repeat(num_scheduled_tokens))
716	        active_lora_requests: set[LoRARequest] = set(
717	            self.lora_id_to_lora_request.values())
718	
719	        return prompt_lora_mapping, token_lora_mapping, active_lora_requests

3	from typing import Callable, Optional

12	def get_token_bin_counts_and_mask(
13	    tokens: torch.Tensor,
14	    vocab_size: int,
15	    num_seqs: int,
16	) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
17	    # Compute the bin counts for the tokens.
18	    # vocab_size + 1 for padding.
19	    bin_counts = torch.zeros((num_seqs, vocab_size + 1),
20	                             dtype=torch.long,
21	                             device=tokens.device)
22	    bin_counts.scatter_add_(1, tokens, torch.ones_like(tokens))
23	    bin_counts = bin_counts[:, :vocab_size]
24	    mask = bin_counts > 0
25	
26	    return bin_counts, mask
27	
28	
29	def apply_penalties(logits: torch.Tensor, prompt_tokens_tensor: torch.Tensor,
30	                    output_tokens_tensor: torch.Tensor,
31	                    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor,
32	                    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor,
33	                    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
34	    """
35	    Applies penalties in place to the logits tensor
36	    logits : The input logits tensor of shape [num_seqs, vocab_size]
37	    prompt_tokens_tensor: A tensor containing the prompt tokens. The prompts 
38	        are padded to the maximum prompt length within the batch using 
39	        `vocab_size` as the padding value. The value `vocab_size` is used 
40	        for padding because it does not correspond to any valid token ID 
41	        in the vocabulary.
42	    output_tokens_tensor: The output tokens tensor.
43	    presence_penalties: The presence penalties of shape (num_seqs, )
44	    frequency_penalties: The frequency penalties of shape (num_seqs, )
45	    repetition_penalties: The repetition penalties of shape (num_seqs, )
46	    """
47	    num_seqs, vocab_size = logits.shape
48	    _, prompt_mask = get_token_bin_counts_and_mask(prompt_tokens_tensor,
49	                                                   vocab_size, num_seqs)
50	    output_bin_counts, output_mask = get_token_bin_counts_and_mask(
51	        output_tokens_tensor, vocab_size, num_seqs)
52	
53	    # Apply repetition penalties as a custom op
54	    from vllm._custom_ops import apply_repetition_penalties
55	    apply_repetition_penalties(logits, prompt_mask, output_mask,
56	                               repetition_penalties)
57	
58	    # We follow the definition in OpenAI API.
59	    # Refer to https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/parameter-details
60	    logits -= frequency_penalties.unsqueeze(dim=1) * output_bin_counts
61	    logits -= presence_penalties.unsqueeze(dim=1) * output_mask
62	    return logits
63	
64	
65	def default_unquantized_gemm(layer: torch.nn.Module,
66	                             x: torch.Tensor,
67	                             weight: torch.Tensor,
68	                             bias: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None):
69	    return torch.nn.functional.linear(x, weight, bias)
70	
71	
72	def rocm_unquantized_gemm(layer: torch.nn.Module,
73	                          x: torch.Tensor,
74	                          weight: torch.Tensor,
75	                          bias: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None):
76	    from vllm.platforms.rocm import on_gfx9
77	    k = weight.shape[1]
78	    use_skinny = (envs.VLLM_ROCM_USE_SKINNY_GEMM and on_gfx9() and \
79	                    x.dtype in [torch.float16, torch.bfloat16] \
80	                    and k % 8 == 0 and bias is None)
81	
82	    if use_skinny is not True:
83	        return torch.nn.functional.linear(x, weight, bias)
84	
85	    x_view = x.view(-1, x.size(-1))
86	    n = x_view.shape[0]
87	    m = weight.shape[0]
88	    cu_count = current_platform.get_cu_count()
89	
90	    if m > 8 and 0 < n <= 4:
91	        out = ops.wvSplitK(weight, x_view, cu_count)
92	        return out.view(*x.shape[:-1], weight.shape[0])
93	    elif m % 4 == 0 and n == 1 and k <= 8192:
94	        out = ops.LLMM1(weight, x_view, 4)
95	        return out.view(*x.shape[:-1], weight.shape[0])
96	    return torch.nn.functional.linear(x, weight, bias)
97	
98	
99	def cpu_unquantized_gemm(layer: torch.nn.Module,
100	                         x: torch.Tensor,
101	                         weight: torch.Tensor,
102	                         bias: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None):
103	    if getattr(layer, "use_cpu_sgl", False):
104	        return torch.ops._C.weight_packed_linear(x, weight, bias, True)
105	    else:
106	        return torch.nn.functional.linear(x, weight, bias)
107	
108	
109	def dispatch_unquantized_gemm() -> Callable[..., torch.Tensor]:
110	    if current_platform.is_rocm():
111	        return rocm_unquantized_gemm
112	    elif current_platform.is_cpu():
113	        return cpu_unquantized_gemm
114	    else:
115	        return default_unquantized_gemm

10	def apply_all_penalties(
11	    logits: torch.Tensor,
12	    prompt_token_ids: torch.Tensor,
13	    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor,
14	    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor,
15	    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor,
16	    output_token_ids: list[list[int]],
17	) -> torch.Tensor:
18	    """
19	    Applies presence, frequency and repetition penalties to the logits.
20	    """
21	    _, vocab_size = logits.shape
22	    output_tokens_t = _convert_to_tensors(output_token_ids, vocab_size,
23	                                          logits.device)
24	    return apply_penalties(logits, prompt_token_ids, output_tokens_t,
25	                           presence_penalties, frequency_penalties,
26	                           repetition_penalties)
27	
28	
29	def _convert_to_tensors(output_token_ids: list[list[int]], vocab_size: int,
30	                        device: torch.device) -> torch.Tensor:
31	    """
32	    Convert the different list data structures to tensors.
33	    """
34	    output_tokens_tensor = make_tensor_with_pad(
35	        output_token_ids,
36	        # Use the value of vocab_size as a pad since we don't have a
37	        # token_id of this value.
38	        pad=vocab_size,
39	        device="cpu",
40	        dtype=torch.int64,
41	        pin_memory=is_pin_memory_available(),
42	    )
43	    return output_tokens_tensor.to(device, non_blocking=True)

6	_SMALLEST_LOGIT = float("-inf")
7	
8	
9	def _apply_bad_words_single_batch(
10	    logits: torch.Tensor,
11	    bad_words_token_ids: list[list[int]],
12	    past_tokens_ids: list[int],
13	) -> None:
14	    for bad_word_ids in bad_words_token_ids:
15	        if len(bad_word_ids) > len(past_tokens_ids) + 1:
16	            continue
17	
18	        prefix_length = len(bad_word_ids) - 1
19	        last_token_id = bad_word_ids[-1]
20	        if prefix_length > 0:
21	            actual_prefix = past_tokens_ids[-prefix_length:]
22	        else:
23	            actual_prefix = []
24	        expected_prefix = bad_word_ids[:prefix_length]
25	
26	        assert len(actual_prefix) == len(expected_prefix)
27	
28	        if actual_prefix == expected_prefix:
29	            logits[last_token_id] = _SMALLEST_LOGIT
30

33	    logits: torch.Tensor,
34	    bad_words_token_ids: dict[int, list[list[int]]],
35	    past_tokens_ids: list[list[int]],
36	) -> None:
37	    for i, bad_words_ids in bad_words_token_ids.items():
38	        _apply_bad_words_single_batch(logits[i], bad_words_ids,
39	                                      past_tokens_ids[i])

37	@dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)
38	class BatchUpdate:
39	    """Persistent batch state change info for logitsprocs"""
40	    batch_size: int  # Current num reqs in batch
41	
42	    # Metadata for requests added to, removed from, and moved
43	    # within the persistent batch.
44	    #
45	    # Note: each added request is represented as
46	    # (index, params, output_tok_ids)
47	    # Key assumption: output_tok_ids is a reference to the
48	    # request's running output tokens list; in this way
49	    # the logits processors always see the latest list of
50	    # generated tokens
51	    removed: Sequence[RemovedRequest]
52	    moved: Sequence[MovedRequest]
53	    added: Sequence[AddedRequest]

56	class BatchUpdateBuilder:
57	    """Helps track persistent batch state changes and build
58	    a batch update data structure for logitsprocs
59	    
60	    Assumptions:
61	    * All information about requests removed from persistent batch
62	      during a step is aggregated in self._removed through calls to
63	      self.removed_append() at the beginning of a step. This must happen
64	      before the first time that self.removed, self.pop_removed()
65	      or self.peek_removed() are invoked in a given step
66	    * After the first time that self.removed, self.pop_removed()
67	      or self.peek_removed() are read in a step, no new removals
68	      are registered using self.removed_append()
69	    * Elements of self._removed are never directly modified, added or
70	      removed (i.e. modification is only via self.removed_append() and
71	      self.pop_removed())
72	    
73	    Guarantees under above assumptions:
74	    * self.removed is always sorted in descending order
75	    * self.pop_removed() and self.peek_removed() both return
76	      the lowest removed request index in the current step
77	    """
78	
79	    _removed: list[RemovedRequest]
80	    _is_removed_sorted: bool
81	    moved: list[MovedRequest]
82	    added: list[AddedRequest]
83	
84	    def __init__(
85	        self,
86	        removed: Optional[list[RemovedRequest]] = None,
87	        moved: Optional[list[MovedRequest]] = None,
88	        added: Optional[list[AddedRequest]] = None,
89	    ) -> None:
90	        self._removed = removed or []
91	        self.moved = moved or []
92	        self.added = added or []
93	        self._is_removed_sorted = False
94	
95	    def _ensure_removed_sorted(self) -> None:
96	        """Sort removed request indices in
97	        descending order.
98	        
99	        Idempotent after first call in a
100	        given step, until reset.
101	        """
102	        if not self._is_removed_sorted:
103	            self._removed.sort(reverse=True)
104	            self._is_removed_sorted = True
105	
106	    @property
107	    def removed(self) -> list[RemovedRequest]:
108	        """Removed request indices sorted in
109	        descending order"""
110	        self._ensure_removed_sorted()
111	        return self._removed
112	
113	    def removed_append(self, index: int) -> None:
114	        """Register the removal of a request from
115	        the persistent batch.
116	
117	        Must not be called after the first time
118	        self.removed, self.pop_removed() or
119	        self.peek_removed() are invoked.
120	        
121	        Args:
122	          index: request index
123	        """
124	        if self._is_removed_sorted:
125	            raise RuntimeError("Cannot register new removed request after"
126	                               " self.removed has been read.")
127	        self._removed.append(index)
128	
129	    def has_removed(self) -> bool:
130	        return bool(self._removed)
131	
132	    def peek_removed(self) -> Optional[int]:
133	        """Return lowest removed request index"""
134	        if self.has_removed():
135	            self._ensure_removed_sorted()
136	            return self._removed[-1]
137	        return None
138	
139	    def pop_removed(self) -> Optional[int]:
140	        """Pop lowest removed request index"""
141	        if self.has_removed():
142	            self._ensure_removed_sorted()
143	            return self._removed.pop()
144	        return None
145	
146	    def get_and_reset(self, batch_size: int) -> Optional[BatchUpdate]:
147	        """Generate a logitsprocs batch update data structure
148	        and reset internal batch update builder state.
149	        
150	        Args:
151	          batch_size: current persistent batch size
152	
153	        Returns:
154	          Frozen logitsprocs batch update instance; `None` if no updates
155	        """
156	        # Reset removal-sorting logic
157	        self._is_removed_sorted = False
158	        if not any((self._removed, self.moved, self.added)):
159	            # No update; short-circuit
160	            return None
161	        # Build batch state update
162	        batch_update = BatchUpdate(
163	            batch_size=batch_size,
164	            removed=self._removed,
165	            moved=self.moved,
166	            added=self.added,
167	        )
168	        # Reset removed/moved/added update lists
169	        self._removed = []
170	        self.moved = []
171	        self.added = []
172	        return batch_update
173	
174	
175	class LogitsProcessor(ABC):
176	
177	    @abstractmethod
178	    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
179	        raise NotImplementedError
180	
181	    @abstractmethod
182	    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
183	        """True if logits processor has no impact on the
184	        argmax computation in greedy sampling.
185	        NOTE: may or may not have the same value for all
186	        instances of a given LogitsProcessor subclass,
187	        depending on subclass implementation.
188	        TODO(andy): won't be utilized until logits
189	        processors are user-extensible
190	        """
191	        raise NotImplementedError
192	
193	    @abstractmethod
194	    def update_state(
195	        self,
196	        batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate],
197	    ) -> None:
198	        """Called when there are new output tokens, prior
199	        to each forward pass.
200	
201	        Args:
202	            batch_update is non-None iff there have been
203	            changes to the batch makeup.
204	        """
205	        raise NotImplementedError
206	
207	
208	@dataclass
209	class LogitsProcessorManager:
210	    """Encapsulates initialized logitsproc objects."""
211	    argmax_invariant: list[LogitsProcessor] = field(
212	        default_factory=list)  # argmax-invariant logitsprocs
213	    non_argmax_invariant: list[LogitsProcessor] = field(
214	        default_factory=list)  # non-argmax-invariant logitsprocs
215	
216	    @property
217	    def all(self) -> Iterator[LogitsProcessor]:
218	        """Iterator over all logits processors."""
219	        return chain(self.argmax_invariant, self.non_argmax_invariant)
220	
221	
222	###### ----- Built-in LogitsProcessor impls below here
223	
224	
225	class MinPLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):
226	
227	    def __init__(self, max_num_reqs: int, pin_memory: bool,
228	                 device: DeviceLikeType):
229	        super().__init__()
230	        self.min_p_count: int = 0
231	
232	        self.min_p_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros((max_num_reqs, ),
233	                                            dtype=torch.float32,
234	                                            device="cpu",
235	                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
236	        self.min_p_cpu = self.min_p_cpu_tensor.numpy()
237	        # Pre-allocated device tensor
238	        self.min_p_device: torch.Tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
239	                                                      dtype=torch.float32,
240	                                                      device=device)
241	        # Current slice of the device tensor
242	        self.min_p: torch.Tensor = self.min_p_device[:0]
243	
244	    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
245	        """Min-p never impacts greedy sampling"""
246	        return True
247	
248	    def get_min_p_by_index(self, index: int) -> float:
249	        return float(self.min_p_cpu[index])
250	
251	    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
252	        if not batch_update:
253	            return
254	
255	        needs_update = False
256	        # Process added requests.
257	        for index, params, _ in batch_update.added:
258	            min_p = params.min_p if isinstance(params, SamplingParams) else 0.0
259	            if self.min_p_cpu[index] != min_p:
260	                needs_update = True
261	                self.min_p_cpu[index] = min_p
262	            if min_p:
263	                self.min_p_count += 1
264	
265	        if self.min_p_count:
266	            # Process removed requests.
267	            needs_update |= bool(batch_update.removed)
268	            for index in batch_update.removed:
269	                if self.min_p_cpu[index]:
270	                    self.min_p_count -= 1
271	
272	            # Process moved requests, unidirectional (a->b) and swap (a<->b)
273	            for adx, bdx, direct in batch_update.moved:
274	                change = (min_p_a :=
275	                          self.min_p_cpu[adx]) != (min_p_b :=
276	                                                   self.min_p_cpu[bdx])
277	                needs_update |= change
278	                if change:
279	                    self.min_p_cpu[bdx] = min_p_a
280	                    if direct == MoveDirectionality.SWAP:
281	                        self.min_p_cpu[adx] = min_p_b
282	
283	        # Update tensors if needed.
284	        size = batch_update.batch_size
285	        if self.min_p_count and (needs_update or self.min_p.shape[0] != size):
286	            self.min_p = self.min_p_device[:size]
287	            self.min_p.copy_(self.min_p_cpu_tensor[:size], non_blocking=True)
288	            self.min_p.unsqueeze_(1)
289	
290	    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
291	        if not self.min_p_count:
292	            return logits
293	
294	        # Convert logits to probability distribution
295	        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
296	        # Calculate maximum probabilities per sequence
297	        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values,
298	                                       dim=-1,
299	                                       keepdim=True)
300	        # Adjust min_p

591:class BeamSearchParams(

109	class RequestOutputKind(Enum):
110	    # Return entire output so far in every RequestOutput
111	    CUMULATIVE = 0
112	    # Return only deltas in each RequestOutput
113	    DELTA = 1
114	    # Do not return intermediate RequestOutput
115	    FINAL_ONLY = 2
116	
117	
118	class SamplingParams(
119	        msgspec.Struct,
120	        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
121	        # required for @cached_property.
122	        dict=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
123	    """Sampling parameters for text generation.
124	
125	    Overall, we follow the sampling parameters from the OpenAI text completion
126	    API (https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/create).
127	    In addition, we support beam search, which is not supported by OpenAI.
128	
129	    Args:
130	        n: Number of output sequences to return for the given prompt.
131	        best_of: Number of output sequences that are generated from the prompt.
132	            From these `best_of` sequences, the top `n` sequences are returned.
133	            `best_of` must be greater than or equal to `n`. By default,
134	            `best_of` is set to `n`. Warning, this is only supported in V0.
135	        presence_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether they
136	            appear in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the model
137	            to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to repeat
138	            tokens.
139	        frequency_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on their
140	            frequency in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the
141	            model to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to
142	            repeat tokens.
143	        repetition_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether
144	            they appear in the prompt and the generated text so far. Values > 1
145	            encourage the model to use new tokens, while values < 1 encourage
146	            the model to repeat tokens.
147	        temperature: Float that controls the randomness of the sampling. Lower
148	            values make the model more deterministic, while higher values make
149	            the model more random. Zero means greedy sampling.
150	        top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens
151	            to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens.
152	        top_k: Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
153	            to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens.
154	        min_p: Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be
155	            considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token.
156	            Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this.
157	        seed: Random seed to use for the generation.
158	        stop: list of strings that stop the generation when they are generated.

## Comprehensive Sampling Parameters Exploration Report

I've completed a thorough exploration of the vLLM sampling parameters system. Here are my findings:

### 1. **Core SamplingParams Class Definition**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775506405255-z9l9l1/vllm/sampling_params.py`

**Class Definition (Lines 118-603):**
- Inherits from `msgspec.Struct` with `omit_defaults=True` and `dict=True`
- Uses msgspec for serialization support

**Key Filtering Parameters with Full Validation:**

| Parameter | Field | Type | Default | Validation (Lines) | Valid Range |
|-----------|-------|------|---------|-----------|-------------|
| `top_p` | Line 212 | `float` | 1.0 | Lines 417-418 | (0, 1] |
| `top_k` | Line 213 | `int` | 0 | Lines 420-425 | >= -1 (int type) |
| `min_p` | Line 214 | `float` | 0.0 | Lines 426-428 | [0, 1] |
| `temperature` | Line 211 | `float` | 1.0 | Lines 414-416, 343-348 | >= 0.0 |
| `presence_penalty` | Line 208 | `float` | 0.0 | Lines 404-406 | [-2.0, 2.0] |
| `frequency_penalty` | Line 209 | `float` | 0.0 | Lines 407-409 | [-2.0, 2.0] |
| `repetition_penalty` | Line 210 | `float` | 1.0 | Lines 410-413 | > 0.0 |

### 2. **SamplingType Enum**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775506405255-z9l9l1/vllm/sampling_params.py` (Lines 24-27)

```python
class SamplingType(IntEnum):
    GREEDY = 0
    RANDOM = 1
    RANDOM_SEED = 2
```

**Determination Logic** (Lines 531-537 - `sampling_type` cached_property):
- `GREEDY`: if `temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS` (1e-5)
- `RANDOM_SEED`: if seed is not None and temperature >= threshold
- `RANDOM`: otherwise

### 3. **V0 Sampling Metadata & Tensor Conversion**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775506405255-z9l9l1/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`

**Intermediate Data Structures:**

1. **SequenceGroupToSample** (Lines 19-62):
   - `sampling_params: SamplingParams` (Line 30)
   - `seq_ids: list[int]` (Line 29)
   - `seq_data: dict[int, SequenceData]` (Line 32)
   - `sample_indices: list[int]` (Line 50)
   - `prompt_logprob_indices: list[int]` (Line 48)

2. **SamplingMetadata** (Lines 97-195):
   - Contains `seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample]`
   - Contains `categorized_sample_indices: dict[SamplingType, torch.Tensor]`
   - Preparation method: `SamplingMetadata.prepare()` (Lines 148-188)

3. **SamplingTensors** (Lines 372-597):
   - **Fields created from SamplingParams:**
     - `temperatures: torch.Tensor` (Line 376)
     - `top_ps: torch.Tensor` (Line 377)
     - `top_ks: torch.Tensor` (Line 378)
     - `min_ps: torch.Tensor` (Line 379)
     - `presence_penalties: torch.Tensor` (Line 380)
     - `frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor` (Line 381)
     - `repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor` (Line 382)
     - `prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor` (Line 383)
     - `output_tokens: torch.Tensor` (Line 384)

**Conversion Flow** (Lines 386-495 - `from_sampling_metadata` classmethod):
- Iterates over `sampling_metadata.seq_groups`
- Extracts parameters from each `seq_group.sampling_params` (Lines 410-416)
- Tracks activation flags:
  - `do_penalties` (Line 403) - set if any penalty != 0
  - `do_top_p_top_k` (Line 404) - set if top_p < 1.0 or top_k != vocab_size
  - `do_min_p` (Line 405) - set if min_p > 1e-5
- Returns tuple: `(sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p)`

**Tensor Creation** (Lines 498-597 - `from_lists` classmethod):
- Creates CPU tensors with pinned memory by default
- Uses dtype parameters:
  - `float` dtype for: temperatures, top_ps, min_ps, penalties
  - `torch.int` dtype for: top_ks
  - `torch.int64` dtype for: prompt_tokens, output_tokens

### 4. **V1 Sampling Metadata & GPU Input Batch**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775506405255-z9l9l1/vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py`

**SamplingMetadata Structure** (Lines 12-43):
```python
@dataclass
class SamplingMetadata:
    temperature: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    all_greedy: bool
    all_random: bool
    top_p: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    top_k: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator]
    max_num_logprobs: Optional[int]
    no_penalties: bool
    prompt_token_ids: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
    output_token_ids: list[list[int]]
    allowed_token_ids_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    bad_words_token_ids: dict[int, list[list[int]]]
    logitsprocs: LogitsProcessorManager
```

**GPU Input Batch** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775506405255-z9l9l1/vllm/v1/worker/gpu_input_batch.py`):

- **Pre-allocated Tensors** (Lines 122-192):
  - `temperature`: float32 tensor (Line 122)
  - `top_p`: float32 tensor (Line 133)
  - `top_k`: int32 tensor (Line 143)
  - `frequency_penalties`: float tensor (Line 157)
  - `presence_penalties`: float tensor (Line 170)
  - `repetition_penalties`: float tensor (Line 182)
  - CPU and GPU versions for each

- **Tracking Sets** for conditional processing:
  - `greedy_reqs: set[str]` (Line 130)
  - `random_reqs: set[str]` (Line 131)
  - `top_p_reqs: set[str]` (Line 141) - when top_p < 1
  - `top_k_reqs: set[str]` (Line 151) - when 0 < top_k < vocab_size
  - `frequency_penalties_reqs: set[str]` (Line 167)
  - `presence_penalties_reqs: set[str]` (Line 179)
  - `repetition_penalties_reqs: set[str]` (Line 192)

- **Parameter Extraction from SamplingParams** (Lines 300-332):
  - Greedy handling: sets temperature to -1.0 (Line 306)
  - Top-p extraction: Line 312
  - Top-k extraction with vocab_size clamping: Lines 315-320
  - Penalty extraction: Lines 321-332

- **Min-P Handling:** NOT in InputBatch, handled separately via `MinPLogitsProcessor`

### 5. **Min-P Logits Processor**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775506405255-z9l9l1/vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py`

**MinPLogitsProcessor Class** (Lines 225-311):
- Maintains `min_p_cpu_tensor: torch.Tensor` (Line 232) - float32
- Maintains `min_p_device: torch.Tensor` (Line 238) - float32
- Tracks `min_p_count: int` (Line 230) - count of active min_p requests
- `update_state()` processes batch updates (Lines 251-288)
- `apply()` filters logits based on minimum probability (Lines 290-311)
- `is_argmax_invariant()` returns True (Lines 244-246)

**State Management** (Lines 251-288):
- Processes added requests (Lines 257-263)
- Processes removed requests (Lines 265-270)
- Handles moved/swapped requests (Lines 272-281)
- Updates device tensor only when needed (Lines 285-288)

### 6. **Helper Constants & Validation**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775506405255-z9l9l1/vllm/sampling_params.py`

- `_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5` (Line 20) - threshold for greedy/zero temperature detection
- `_MAX_TEMP = 1e-2` (Line 21) - minimum temperature to avoid numerical errors

### 7. **Serialization Support**

- **SamplingParams** uses `msgspec.Struct` for efficient serialization
- **msgspec_utils** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775506405255-z9l9l1/vllm/executor/msgspec_utils.py`):
  - Custom `encode_hook()` for array types
  - Custom `decode_hook()` for deserialization
  - Used for Ray distributed executor communication

### 8. **Additional Enums & Classes**

**RequestOutputKind Enum** (Lines 109-115):
- `CUMULATIVE = 0` - return entire output
- `DELTA = 1` - return only deltas
- `FINAL_ONLY = 2` - no intermediate output

**GuidedDecodingParams Dataclass** (Lines 31-107):
- Supports: json, regex, choice, grammar, json_object
- Backend configuration with deprecated format parsing
- Fields: backend, disable_fallback, disable_any_whitespace, disable_additional_properties, whitespace_pattern, structural_tag

**BeamSearchParams** (Lines 591-602):
- Separate msgspec.Struct for beam search
- Fields: beam_width, max_tokens, ignore_eos, temperature, length_penalty, include_stop_str_in_output

### Summary of Key Validation Points

1. **top_p validation:** Must be in range (0, 1] - checked at line 417
2. **top_k validation:** Must be >= -1 and be integer type - checked at lines 420-425
3. **min_p validation:** Must be in range [0, 1] - checked at line 426
4. **temperature handling:** 
   - Must be >= 0.0 (line 414)
   - Automatically clamped to _MAX_TEMP if 0 < temp < _MAX_TEMP (lines 343-348)
   - Used to determine SamplingType (line 533)
5. **Penalty validations:** presence_penalty and frequency_penalty in [-2, 2], repetition_penalty > 0
6. **Greedy sampling requirement:** When temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS, n must be 1 (line 465)

All file paths are absolute and refer to the vLLM project directory structure.

## Comprehensive vLLM Sampling/Logits Processing Pipeline Report

Based on my thorough exploration of the vLLM codebase, here is a complete map of the sampling and logits processing pipeline:

### 1. **Main Sampler Implementations**

- **File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775506405255-z9l9l1/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`

  - `forward()` - Lines 225-334: Main entry point, coordinates the entire sampling pipeline
  - `_init_sampling_tensors()` - Lines 199-223: Initializes GPU tensors from sampling metadata

#### V1 Sampler (new implementation)
- **File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775506405255-z9l9l1/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py`
- **Key Class**: `Sampler` (nn.Module) - Lines 17-76
- **Key Methods**:
  - `forward()` - Lines 24-76: V1 sampling pipeline
  - `sample()` - Lines 89-136: Orchestrates sampling decisions

#### TPU-Specific Sampler
- **File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775506405255-z9l9l1/vllm/v1/sample/tpu/sampler.py`
- **Key Methods**:
  - `apply_min_p()` - Lines 125-145: Min-P filtering implementation optimized for TPU

---

### 2. **Top-K, Top-P, Min-P Filtering (GPU Tensor Operations)**

#### V0 Main Sampler Functions

**File**: `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`

| Function | Lines | Purpose |
|----------|-------|---------|
| `_apply_top_k_top_p()` | 398-424 | GPU-side filtering for both top-k and top-p with sorting |
| `_apply_min_p()` | 427-441 | Min-P adaptive threshold filtering |
| `_apply_min_tokens_penalty()` | 351-395 | Penalizes stop tokens until min_tokens reached |

**Key Implementation Details**:
- `_apply_top_k_top_p()` (line 398):
  - Sorts logits in ascending order
  - Applies top-k mask using `gather()`
  - Converts top-k logits to mask and sets non-top-k to `-inf`
  - Applies top-p by computing cumulative probabilities
  - Uses `softmax()` and `cumsum()` on sorted logits
  - Re-scatters masked logits back to original indices

- `_apply_min_p()` (line 427):
  - Computes `softmax()` to get probabilities
  - Finds `max(probs)` across vocab
  - Computes `scaled_min_p = min_p * max_prob` per sequence
  - Sets logits where `prob < scaled_min_p` to `-inf`

#### V1 TopKTopP Sampler
**File**: `vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py`

| Class/Function | Lines | Platform | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `TopKTopPSampler` | 22-118 | Multi-platform | Router class dispatching to platform-specific impls |
| `forward_native()` | 69-83 | CPU/PyTorch | Pure PyTorch implementation |
| `forward_cuda()` | 85-107 | CUDA with FlashInfer | Optimized CUDA path |
| `forward_tpu()` | 109-118 | TPU | TPU-optimized implementation |
| `apply_top_k_top_p()` | 168-208 | PyTorch | Sorts logits, applies both filters with rescatter |
| `apply_top_k_only()` | 211-233 | PyTorch | Optimized top-k-only path (no full sort) |
| `apply_top_k_top_p_tpu()` | 121-165 | TPU | Avoids `torch.scatter` (slow on TPU) |
| `flashinfer_sample()` | 260-296 | CUDA | Calls FlashInfer library sampling |
| `random_sample()` | 236-257 | PyTorch | Exponential sampling without CPU sync |

**Key Details**:
- `apply_top_k_top_p()` uses sorting strategy: O(vocab_size * log(vocab_size))
- `apply_top_k_only()` uses `topk()` operator: O(vocab_size) per batch
- TPU version avoids scatter operations by using cumulative probabilities and cut-off thresholds
- FlashInfer path (when available) integrates directly into sampling

---

### 3. **Logits Processors (CPU-side, applied per-sequence)**

#### V0 Logits Processor (CPU)

2. Extracts `SamplingParams` fields: `top_p`, `top_k`, `min_p`, penalties, temperature
3. Builds Python lists: `top_ps`, `top_ks`, `min_ps`, etc.
4. Detects if filtering needed via flags: `do_top_p_top_k`, `do_min_p`, `do_penalties`
5. Calls `from_lists()` to create GPU tensors

**Tensor Creation** (line 498-597):
- Uses **pinned memory** (host → device transfer is non-blocking)
- Creates tensors on CPU with `pin_memory=True`
- Transfers to device via `non_blocking=True` transfer
- Benefits from async GPU transfers during computation

### 5. **CUDA Kernels for Sampling**

**File**: `csrc/sampler.cu`

**Kernel**: `apply_repetition_penalties_kernel` (lines 14-45)

| Kernel | Lines | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `apply_repetition_penalties_kernel` | 14-45 | GPU kernel for repetition penalty |
| `apply_repetition_penalties_()` | 49-88 | PyTorch wrapper binding |

**Key Details**:
- Block-per-sequence, tile-per-vocab parallelization
- Each block handles one sequence and vocab tile
- Applies penalty: `if logit > 0: logit /= penalty else logit *= penalty`
- Dynamically computes tile size based on SMs and sequence count
- Called from `apply_penalties()` in `vllm/model_executor/layers/utils.py` (line 54)

---

### 6. **Penalty Application Pipeline**

**File**: `vllm/model_executor/layers/utils.py`

| Function | Lines | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `apply_penalties()` | 29-62 | Master penalty application function |
| `get_token_bin_counts_and_mask()` | 12-26 | Creates frequency masks from token tensors |

**apply_penalties() Pipeline** (line 29):
1. Accepts `logits`, `prompt_tokens_tensor`, `output_tokens_tensor`, `presence_penalties`, `frequency_penalties`, `repetition_penalties`
2. Computes bin counts and masks for prompt/output tokens
3. Calls CUDA kernel `apply_repetition_penalties()` for repetition penalty
4. Applies frequency penalty: `logits -= frequency_penalties * output_bin_counts`
5. Applies presence penalty: `logits -= presence_penalties * output_mask`

---

### 7. **Sampling Metadata & Batch Structure**

**File**: `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`

**Related Classes**:
- `SamplingMetadata` - Batch-level sampling metadata
- `SequenceGroupToSample` - Per-sequence-group sampling parameters
- `SamplingTensors` - GPU tensors derived from sampling params (covered above)

**Data Flow**:
```
SamplingParams (per request)
  ↓
SequenceGroupToSample (grouped)
  ↓
SamplingMetadata (batch)
  ↓
SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata() 
  ↓
GPU Tensors (ready for filtering)
```

### 8. **V0 Sampler Main Forward Pass** 

**File**: `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` - Line 225-334

**Execution Order**:
1. **Line 254-261**: Initialize sampling tensors (GPU-resident with pinned memory)
2. **Line 269**: Apply min_tokens penalty (CPU-side, penalizes stop tokens)
3. **Line 272-277**: Apply presence/frequency/repetition penalties (GPU kernel + tensor ops)
4. **Line 281-282**: Temperature scaling (in-place division)
5. **Line 284-286**: Apply top-k/top-p filtering (if FlashInfer unavailable)
6. **Line 288-289**: Apply min-p filtering
7. **Line 293-295**: Compute softmax probabilities and log-probabilities
8. **Line 298-305**: Sample tokens (greedy or multinomial)
9. **Line 318-334**: Build sampler output with optional logprobs

---

### 9. **Key Constants & Thresholds**

**File**: `vllm/sampling_params.py`

| Constant | Lines | Value | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `_SAMPLING_EPS` | 19, 20 | 1e-5 | Epsilon for float comparisons |
| `_MAX_TEMP` | 20 | 1e-2 | Max temperature for deterministic sampling |

**Validation** (lines 417-428):
- `top_p` must be in `(0, 1]`
- `top_k` must be `>= -1` (0 or -1 disables)
- `min_p` must be in `[0, 1]`

### 10. **FlashInfer Integration**

**File**: `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`

| Integration Point | Lines | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| Import FlashInfer | 25-32 | Conditional import of FlashInfer sampler |
| `_top_k_top_p_multinomial_with_flashinfer()` | 550-562 | Wrapper for FlashInfer sampling |
| V1 `forward_cuda()` | 85-107 | Routes to FlashInfer when available |

**Details**:
- FlashInfer 0.2.3+ required
- Requires contiguous logits (line 107 notes flex_attn can produce non-contiguous)
- Provides rejection sampling for better statistical properties
- CPU-GPU sync point (unlike PyTorch native `random_sample()`)

### 11. **File Structure Summary**

**Core Sampling Files**:
```
vllm/
├── model_executor/
│   ├── layers/
│   │   ├── sampler.py (V0 Sampler - main)

├── v1/

│       ├── logits_processor.py (V1 built-in logits processors)
│       ├── ops/

│       │   ├── penalties.py (V1 penalty application)

```

### 12. **Complete Pipeline Summary**

**Data Flow from SamplingParams → Sampled Tokens**:

```
SamplingParams (user input)
    ↓
SamplingMetadata (batch aggregation)
    ↓
SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()
```

### 13. **Function Call Chain Summary**

| `apply_top_k_top_p_tpu()` | 121-165 | TPU | Avoids `torch.scatter` (slow on TPU) |
| `flashinfer_sample()` | 260-296 | CUDA | Calls FlashInfer library sampling |
| `random_sample()` | 236-257 | PyTorch | Exponential sampling without CPU sync |

**Key Details**:
- `apply_top_k_top_p()` uses sorting strategy: O(vocab_size * log(vocab_size))
- `apply_top_k_only()` uses `topk()` operator: O(vocab_size) per batch
- TPU version avoids scatter operations by using cumulative probabilities and cut-off thresholds
- FlashInfer path (when available) integrates directly into sampling

### 5. **CUDA Kernels for Sampling**

**Key Details**:
- Block-per-sequence, tile-per-vocab parallelization
- Each block handles one sequence and vocab tile
- Applies penalty: `if logit > 0: logit /= penalty else logit *= penalty`
- Dynamically computes tile size based on SMs and sequence count

1. Accepts `logits`, `prompt_tokens_tensor`, `output_tokens_tensor`, `presence_penalties`, `frequency_penalties`, `repetition_penalties`
2. Computes bin counts and masks for prompt/output tokens
3. Calls CUDA kernel `apply_repetition_penalties()` for repetition penalty
4. Applies frequency penalty: `logits -= frequency_penalties * output_bin_counts`
5. Applies presence penalty: `logits -= presence_penalties * output_mask`

# vLLM Sampling Parameters Flow Analysis: min_p (Template for top_a)

## Overview
This document captures a complete trace of how sampling parameter `min_p` flows through vLLM's data structures from the OpenAI API all the way to the GPU sampler layer. This is the blueprint for implementing `top_a` (Top-Alternative tokens), as top_a follows the exact same pattern as min_p.

---

## 1. CORE DATA STRUCTURES

### 1.1 SamplingParams (Main container for all sampling params)
**File**: `/vllm/sampling_params.py`
**Class**: `SamplingParams` (msgspec.Struct)

Key lines for min_p:
- **Line 154-156**: Documentation describing min_p parameter
- **Line 214**: Field definition: `min_p: float = 0.0`
- **Line 261**: Parameter in `from_optional()` constructor: `min_p: float = 0.0`
- **Line 304**: Passed through to constructor in `from_optional()`
- **Line 381**: Reset to 0.0 when temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS (greedy sampling)
- **Line 426-428**: Validation: `if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0`
- **Line 572**: Included in `__repr__()` method

**Pattern**: Simple float field, validated to [0, 1] range, reset on greedy.

---

### 1.2 OpenAI Protocol Layer
**File**: `/vllm/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`

#### ChatCompletionRequest
- **Line 262**: Field definition: `min_p: Optional[float] = None`

#### to_sampling_params() method (line 452-542)
- **Line 474-476**: Default handling:
  ```python
  if (min_p := self.min_p) is None:
      min_p = default_sampling_params.get("min_p", self._DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["min_p"])
  ```
- **Line 521**: Passed to SamplingParams.from_optional(): `min_p=min_p,`

**Pattern**: Optional field in API request, defaults to None, gets filled with defaults, passed through to SamplingParams.

---

## 2. METADATA STRUCTURES

### 2.1 V0 SamplingMetadata (for GPU path)
**File**: `/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`

#### SequenceGroupToSample (line 20-62)
- **Line 30**: Contains: `sampling_params: SamplingParams` (the whole object)

#### SamplingTensors (line 372-597)
Used to pack sampling params into tensors for GPU operations.

- **Line 379**: Tensor field: `min_ps: torch.Tensor`
- **Line 399**: In `from_sampling_metadata()` - collected from seq_groups:
  ```python
  min_ps: list[float] = []
  ```
- **Line 416**: Extract from sampling_params:
  ```python

  ```
- **Line 429-430**: Check if min_p is active:
  ```python

      do_min_p = True
  ```
- **Line 446**: For prompt logprobs (replicated to prefill_len):
  ```python

  ```
- **Line 457**: For sample indices (replicated to sample_lens):
  ```python

  ```
- **Line 551-556**: Create tensor:
  ```python

  ```
- **Line 588**: Move to device:
  ```python
  min_ps=min_ps_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
  ```

**Return value** (line 495): `(sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p)`

**Pattern**: Individual float values collected into lists, then converted to tensor, moved to GPU.

---

### 2.2 V1 SamplingMetadata (for TPU/advanced path)
**File**: `/vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py`

#### SamplingMetadata (line 12-43)

### 2.3 TPU Metadata & Input Batch

#### TPU InputBatch (line 20-189)

  ```python
  self.min_p_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ), dtype=torch.float32, device="cpu", pin_memory=pin_memory)
  ```

  ```python

  ```

## 3. SAMPLER LAYER (GPU Side)

### 3.1 V0 Sampler (GPU/CUDA)
**File**: `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`

#### In forward():
- **Line 216-223**: Initialize tensors:
  ```python
  (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p) = SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(
      sampling_metadata, vocab_size, logits.device, logits.dtype)
  self._sampling_tensors = sampling_tensors
  self._do_min_p = do_min_p
  ```

#### In _sample():
- **Line 267**: Get flag: `do_min_p = self._do_min_p`
- **Line 288-289**: Apply if needed:
  ```python
  if do_min_p:
      logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)
  ```

#### Implementation: _apply_min_p() (line 427-441)
```python
def _apply_min_p(logits: torch.Tensor, min_p: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

**Algorithm**:
1. Convert logits to probabilities
2. Find max probability per sequence
3. Scale min_p by max probability
4. Mask tokens below threshold
5. Return modified logits

---

### 3.2 V1 Sampler (TPU)
**File**: `/vllm/v1/sample/tpu/sampler.py`

#### In sample() (line 50-76):
- **Line 62-64**: Apply min_p if present:
  ```python
  if sampling_metadata.min_p is not None:
      logits = self.apply_min_p(logits, sampling_metadata.min_p)
  ```

#### Implementation: apply_min_p() (line 125-145)
```python
def apply_min_p(self, logits: torch.Tensor, min_p: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
    probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values, dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    adjusted_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze(1) * max_probabilities
    valid_token_mask = probability_values >= adjusted_min_p
    logits.masked_fill_(~valid_token_mask, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

**Similar algorithm to V0** (same logic, slightly different implementation for XLA compatibility)

---

### 3.3 V1 Logits Processor Layer (Advanced)
**File**: `/vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py`

#### MinPLogitsProcessor class (line 225-306)
This is an alternative/complement to the sampler implementation.

**State tracking** (line 227-242):
```python
self.min_p_count: int = 0
self.min_p_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros((max_num_reqs, ), dtype=torch.float32, device="cpu", pin_memory=pin_memory)
self.min_p_cpu = self.min_p_cpu_tensor.numpy()
self.min_p_device: torch.Tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ), dtype=torch.float32, device=device)
self.min_p: torch.Tensor = self.min_p_device[:0]
```

**Update state** (line 251-288):
- Process added requests (line 257-263): Set min_p from params, count active
- Process removed requests (line 265-270): Decrement count
- Process moved/swapped requests (line 272-281): Track parameter changes
- Sync to device tensor (line 284-288): Copy CPU to GPU when needed

**Apply** (line 290-306):
```python
def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
    if not self.min_p_count:
        return logits
    probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values, dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    adjusted_min_p = max_probabilities.mul_(self.min_p)
    invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_min_p
    logits[invalid_token_mask] = -float('inf')
    return logits
```

**Properties**:
- **Line 244-246**: `is_argmax_invariant() -> True` (min_p doesn't affect greedy)
- **Line 248-249**: Getter for min_p by index

## 4. REQUEST FLOW TRACE

Complete path from API request to sampled token:

   - Line 257-263: Process added requests
   - Line 265-270: Process removed requests
   - Line 272-281: Process moved requests
   - Line 284-288: Sync to device
   - Line 290-306: apply() method
   - Line 244-246: is_argmax_invariant() property

### Tests:
10. **tests/v1/sample/test_sampling_params_e2e.py**
    - min_p=0.5 usage

11. **tests/v1/sample/test_logits_processors.py**
    - Line: kwargs with min_p=0.0

    - _min_p_validate() function

### Model-Specific:

    - Check for min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS

---

## 6. KEY PATTERNS FOR IMPLEMENTING top_a

Based on min_p implementation, top_a should follow:

1. **SamplingParams (sampling_params.py)**
   - Add: `top_a: float = 0.0`
   - Add validation: `0.0 <= top_a <= 1.0`
   - Add to __repr__() and from_optional()
   - Add documentation in docstring

2. **Protocol (protocol.py)**
   - Add: `top_a: Optional[float] = None` to ChatCompletionRequest
   - Add default handling in to_sampling_params()
   - Pass to SamplingParams.from_optional()

3. **Config (config.py)**
   - Add "top_a" to available_params list (line 1370-1377)

4. **SamplingMetadata (sampling_metadata.py)**
   - Add: `top_as: torch.Tensor` field to SamplingTensors
   - Extract from sampling_params in from_sampling_metadata()
   - Create tensor and transfer to GPU like min_ps
   - Add do_top_a flag to return tuple

5. **V0 Sampler (sampler.py)**
   - Add check: `if do_top_a: logits = _apply_top_a(logits, sampling_tensors.top_as)`
   - Implement _apply_top_a() function with similar algorithm to _apply_min_p()

6. **V1 TPU (tpu_input_batch.py + tpu/sampler.py)**
   - Add tensors similar to min_p
   - Add to InputBatch initialization
   - Add to add_request() population
   - Add getter/properties
   - Add to TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata
   - Add apply_top_a() method to Sampler

7. **V1 Logits Processor (logits_processor.py)**
   - Add TopALogitsProcessor class (similar to MinPLogitsProcessor)
   - Track state, update_state(), apply() methods
   - Register in LogitsProcessorManager

---

## 7. ALGORITHM DETAILS

### min_p Algorithm (Relative Probability Threshold)
```
1. Compute softmax(logits) → probs
2. Find max(probs) → max_prob
3. Compute threshold = min_p * max_prob
4. Mask tokens where prob < threshold (set to -inf)
5. Apply top_k/top_p on remaining tokens
```

### top_a Algorithm (Top Alternative - variant of relative probability)
Expected algorithm (similar concept, different calculation):
```
1. Compute softmax(logits) → probs
2. Find max(probs) → max_prob
3. Compute threshold = max_prob * top_a
4. Mask tokens where prob < threshold (set to -inf)
5. Apply top_k/top_p on remaining tokens
```

Both use relative probability thresholding. Key difference:
- **min_p**: Minimum probability relative to max = min_p * P(max)
- **top_a**: Alternative tokens probability relative to max = top_a * P(max)

---

## 8. VALIDATION REQUIREMENTS

From sampling_params.py validation (line 426-428):
```python
if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
    raise ValueError("min_p must be in [0, 1], got " f"{self.min_p}.")
```

**For top_a**: Use identical validation range [0, 1]

---

## 9. DEFAULT VALUES

From vllm/v1/sample/tpu/metadata.py (line 10-19):
```python
DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS = dict(
    temperature=-1.0,
    min_p=0.0,  # <-- PATTERN
    top_k=0,
    top_p=1.0,
)
```

**For top_a**: Default = 0.0 (disabled)

From sampling_params.py line 214:
```python
min_p: float = 0.0
```

**For top_a**: Default = 0.0 (disabled)

---

## 10. TEMPERATURE INTERACTION

From sampling_params.py line 377-382:
```python
if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
    # Zero temperature means greedy sampling.
    self.top_p = 1.0
    self.top_k = 0
    self.min_p = 0.0
    self._verify_greedy_sampling()
```

**For top_a**: Should also be reset to 0.0 when temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS

---

|-----------|------|-----------|---------|
| **API Input** | protocol.py | 262, 474-476, 521 | Optional field, defaults, pass-through |
| **Core Struct** | sampling_params.py | 154-156, 214, 261, 304, 381, 426-428, 572 | Float field, validate [0,1], reset on greedy |

---

extra_args: Arbitrary additional args, that can be used by custom
            sampling implementations, plugins, etc. Not used by any in-tree
            sampling implementations.
    """

    n: int = 1
    best_of: Optional[int] = None
    _real_n: Optional[int] = None
    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
    repetition_penalty: float = 1.0
    temperature: float = 1.0
    top_p: float = 1.0
    top_k: int = 0
    min_p: float = 0.0
    seed: Optional[int] = None
    stop: Optional[Union[str, list[str]]] = None
    stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
    ignore_eos: bool = False
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16
    min_tokens: int = 0
    logprobs: Optional[int] = None
    prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None
    # NOTE: This parameter is only exposed at the engine level for now.
    # It is not exposed in the OpenAI API server, as the OpenAI API does
    # not support returning only a list of token IDs.
    detokenize: bool = True
    skip_special_tokens: bool = True
    spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True
    # Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] type. We use Any here because
    # Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] type is not supported by msgspec.
    logits_processors: Optional[Any] = None
    include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False
    truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int, msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None
    output_kind: RequestOutputKind = RequestOutputKind.CUMULATIVE

    # The below fields are not supposed to be used as an input.
    # They are set in post_init.
    output_text_buffer_length: int = 0
    _all_stop_token_ids: set[int] = msgspec.field(default_factory=set)

    # Fields used to construct logits processors
    guided_decoding: Optional[GuidedDecodingParams] = None
    logit_bias: Optional[dict[int, float]] = None
    allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
    extra_args: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None

    # Fields used for bad words
    bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None
    _bad_words_token_ids: Optional[list[list[int]]] = None

    def from_optional(
        n: Optional[int] = 1,
        best_of: Optional[int] = None,
        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = 0.0,
        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = 0.0,
        repetition_penalty: Optional[float] = 1.0,
        temperature: Optional[float] = 1.0,
        top_p: Optional[float] = 1.0,
        top_k: int = 0,
        min_p: float = 0.0,
        seed: Optional[int] = None,

        stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None,
        bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None,
        include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False,
        ignore_eos: bool = False,
        max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16,
        min_tokens: int = 0,
        logprobs: Optional[int] = None,
        prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None,
        detokenize: bool = True,
        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
        logits_processors: Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] = None,
        truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int,
                                                   msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None,
        output_kind: RequestOutputKind = RequestOutputKind.CUMULATIVE,

        logit_bias: Optional[Union[dict[int, float], dict[str, float]]] = None,
        allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None,
        extra_args: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
    ) -> "SamplingParams":
        if logit_bias is not None:
            # Convert token_id to integer
            # Clamp the bias between -100 and 100 per OpenAI API spec

# speculative decoding and when prompt embeddings are specified.
        self.include_gpu_probs_tensor = False
        self.should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace = False

    def _init_sampling_tensors(
        self,
        logits: torch.Tensor,
        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
    ):
        """The goal here is to reuse sampling tensors between similar decode
        runs. This is possible because sampling logic does not change between
        decodes of the same sequences.
        """
        _, vocab_size = logits.shape

        # First free any existing stored sampling tensors.
        # This is necessary because some sampling tensors may
        # have pinned memory.
        self._sampling_tensors = None

        # Initialize new sampling tensors
        (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k,
         do_min_p) = SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(
             sampling_metadata, vocab_size, logits.device, logits.dtype)

        self._sampling_tensors = sampling_tensors
        self._do_penalties = do_penalties
        self._do_top_p_top_k = do_top_p_top_k
        self._do_min_p = do_min_p

431	    """
432	    Adapted from
433	    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
434	    """
435	    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
436	    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
437	    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
438	    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
439	    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
440	
441	    return logits

file.content:
def _apply_min_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    min_p: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Adapted from
    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
    """
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))

    return logits

372	@dataclass
373	class SamplingTensors:
374	    """Tensors for sampling."""
375	
376	    temperatures: torch.Tensor
377	    top_ps: torch.Tensor
378	    top_ks: torch.Tensor
379	    min_ps: torch.Tensor
380	    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
381	    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
382	    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
383	    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
384	    output_tokens: torch.Tensor
385	
386	    @classmethod
387	    def from_sampling_metadata(
388	        cls,
389	        sampling_metadata: "SamplingMetadata",
390	        vocab_size: int,
391	        device: torch.device,
392	        dtype: torch.dtype,
393	    ) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool]:
394	        prompt_tokens: list[array] = []
395	        output_tokens: list[array] = []
396	        top_ks: list[int] = []
397	        temperatures: list[float] = []
398	        top_ps: list[float] = []
399	        min_ps: list[float] = []
400	        presence_penalties: list[float] = []
401	        frequency_penalties: list[float] = []
402	        repetition_penalties: list[float] = []
403	        do_penalties = False
404	        do_top_p_top_k = False
405	        do_min_p = False
406	
407	        assert sampling_metadata.seq_groups is not None
408	        for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
409	            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
410	            sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
411	            temperature = sampling_params.temperature
412	            p = sampling_params.presence_penalty
413	            f = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
414	            r = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
415	            top_p = sampling_params.top_p
416	            min_p = sampling_params.min_p
417	
418	            # k should not be greater than the vocab size.
419	            top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
420	            top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
421	            if temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
422	                # NOTE: Zero temperature means deterministic sampling
423	                # (i.e., greedy sampling or beam search).
424	                # Set the temperature to 1 to avoid division by zero.
425	                temperature = 1.0
426	            if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
427	                                       or top_k != vocab_size):
428	                do_top_p_top_k = True
429	            if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
430	                do_min_p = True
431	            if not do_penalties and (abs(p) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
432	                                     or abs(f) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
433	                                     or abs(r - 1.0) >= _SAMPLING_EPS):
434	                do_penalties = True
435	
436	            is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
437	            if is_prompt and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
438	                # For tokens in the prompt that we only need to get
439	                # their logprobs
440	                query_len = seq_group.query_len
441	                assert query_len is not None
442	                prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
443	                temperatures += [temperature] * prefill_len
444	                top_ps += [top_p] * prefill_len
445	                top_ks += [top_k] * prefill_len
446	                min_ps += [min_p] * prefill_len

@dataclass
class SamplingTensors:
    """Tensors for sampling."""

    temperatures: torch.Tensor
    top_ps: torch.Tensor
    top_ks: torch.Tensor
    min_ps: torch.Tensor
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
    output_tokens: torch.Tensor

    @classmethod
    def from_sampling_metadata(
        cls,
        sampling_metadata: "SamplingMetadata",
        vocab_size: int,
        device: torch.device,
        dtype: torch.dtype,
    ) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool]:
        prompt_tokens: list[array] = []
        output_tokens: list[array] = []
        top_ks: list[int] = []
        temperatures: list[float] = []
        top_ps: list[float] = []
        min_ps: list[float] = []
        presence_penalties: list[float] = []
        frequency_penalties: list[float] = []
        repetition_penalties: list[float] = []
        do_penalties = False
        do_top_p_top_k = False
        do_min_p = False

        assert sampling_metadata.seq_groups is not None
        for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
            sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
            temperature = sampling_params.temperature
            p = sampling_params.presence_penalty
            f = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
            r = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
            top_p = sampling_params.top_p
            min_p = sampling_params.min_p

            # k should not be greater than the vocab size.
            top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
            top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
            if temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
                # NOTE: Zero temperature means deterministic sampling
                # (i.e., greedy sampling or beam search).
                # Set the temperature to 1 to avoid division by zero.
                temperature = 1.0
            if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
                                       or top_k != vocab_size):
                do_top_p_top_k = True
            if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
                do_min_p = True
            if not do_penalties and (abs(p) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
                                     or abs(f) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
                                     or abs(r - 1.0) >= _SAMPLING_EPS):
                do_penalties = True

            is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
            if is_prompt and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
                # For tokens in the prompt that we only need to get
                # their logprobs
                query_len = seq_group.query_len
                assert query_len is not None
                prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
                temperatures += [temperature] * prefill_len
                top_ps += [top_p] * prefill_len
                top_ks += [top_k] * prefill_len
                min_ps += [min_p] * prefill_len
                presence_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
                frequency_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
                repetition_penalties += [1] * prefill_len

            if seq_group.do_sample:
                sample_lens = len(seq_group.sample_indices)
                assert sample_lens >= len(seq_ids)
                temperatures += [temperature] * sample_lens
                top_ps += [top_p] * sample_lens
                top_ks += [top_k] * sample_lens
                min_ps += [min_p] * sample_lens
                presence_penalties += [p] * sample_lens
                frequency_penalties += [f] * sample_lens
                repetition_penalties += [r] * sample_lens

        if do_penalties:
            for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
                seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
                sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
                if (seq_group.is_prompt
                        and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None):
                    prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
                    prompt_tokens.extend(
                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
                    output_tokens.extend(
                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
                if seq_group.do_sample:
                    for seq_id in seq_ids:
                        seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
                        prompt_tokens.append(seq_data.prompt_token_ids_array)
                        output_tokens.append(seq_data.output_token_ids_array)

        sampling_tensors = SamplingTensors.from_lists(
            temperatures,
            top_ps,
            top_ks,
            min_ps,
            presence_penalties,
            frequency_penalties,
            repetition_penalties,
            prompt_tokens,
            output_tokens,
            vocab_size,
            device,
            dtype,
        )
        return (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p)

    @classmethod
    def from_lists(
        cls,
        temperatures: list[float],
        top_ps: list[float],
        top_ks: list[int],
        min_ps: list[float],
        presence_penalties: list[float],
